target.title: fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs
target.body:
## Summary

Extend the subagent yield-pause fix from [6f3b5f86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/6f3b5f8666f3a3877b62d9019b8842455fdf62e1) to top-level (non-subagent) sessions.

When a main session ends via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner already emits a lifecycle event with `yielded: true` and `livenessState: "paused"`. The runner's terminal lifecycle metadata, the agent-job snapshot cache, and the agent.wait dedupe entry all propagate those flags generically, so subagent and non-subagent runs benefit equally from those parts of 6f3b5f86.

The gap is in `gateway/session-lifecycle-state.ts`. `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` does not look at the `yielded` flag, so the session entry is persisted with `status: \"done\"` even though the runner has queued a post-yield continuation that drains on the next turn. Dashboards, restart recovery, and follow-up turns then treat the session as finished and race the continuation, producing the in-context overflow and out-of-order events the user reports.

## Why the same fix needs to apply to the top-level session

The subagent registry tracks active subagent runs in memory and uses `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` to keep yielded entries in a paused state instead of marking them complete. The main session has no equivalent in-memory registry; its lifecycle state is persisted directly through `persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent` into the on-disk session entry. The gap shows up in three places:

1. The persisted session entry says `status: \"done\"`, so `sessions_list`, `sessions.changed` broadcasts, and dashboard UI all show the session as finished.
2. `main-session-restart-recovery` is keyed off `entry.abortedLastRun` and `entry.status === \"running\"`. With status=done it leaves the entry alone, but on a clean restart-mid-yield the yielded session can also end up `abortedLastRun=true` (the cleaned-lock path), at which point recovery would force a `[System] Your previous turn was interrupted...` resume that races the queued continuation.
3. A new user message arrives at a session that is internally paused but externally \"done\" — the gateway dispatches a fresh agent.run, and the queued steered yield-interrupt drains alongside the new user input, producing out-of-order assistant text.

## Shape of the change

- Recognize the lifecycle `yielded` flag in `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` and derive `status: \"paused\"` with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` instead of `done`. Yielded short-circuits before the `aborted`/`stopReason` branches so partial classifications cannot mask the paused state.
- Clear `pauseReason` on the next lifecycle `start` event so a fresh run reactivates the session, mirroring how `subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts` clears `pauseReason` on completion.
- Add `\"paused\"` to `SessionRunStatus` in both `gateway/session-utils.types.ts` and `agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts`, and add `pauseReason?: \"sessions_yield\"` to `SessionEntry` and `GatewaySessionRow`.
- Surface `pauseReason` on the `sessions.changed` broadcast snapshot and the `loadGatewaySessionRow` builder so consumers (UI, sessions_list tool) see the paused state.
- Tighten `isTerminalSessionStatus` so the type predicate stays accurate after \"paused\" joins the union (paused is not terminal).
- Skip `main-session-restart-recovery` for entries with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` so they are not re-prompted with the generic interrupted-turn message.

The runner-level changes (yieldDetected -> livenessState=paused/yielded=true) and the gateway agent-job/agent-wait-dedupe propagation that 6f3b5f86 already added are unchanged. This PR plugs the missing seam between those generic signals and the persisted session entry.

## Test coverage

- `src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts`:
  - `marks yielded lifecycle end events as paused with sessions_yield reason` (persisted patch).
  - `treats yielded as paused even when aborted is set` (yield short-circuit).
  - `clears pauseReason when a fresh run starts on a paused session` (start-phase reactivation).
  - Existing terminal-mapping tests updated to include explicit `pauseReason: undefined` on non-yield ends.
- `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`:
  - `skips sessions paused via sessions_yield instead of forcing a resume`.
  - `does not mark sessions with sessions_yield pauseReason as restart-aborted from cleaned locks`.

## Local validation

- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — clean.
- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/config/sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.create-event.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` — all green (235 tests).
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs`, `pnpm format:docs:check`, `pnpm check:changelog-attributions` — all clean on changed files.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on \`fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs\`.
- [ ] Manual smoke: trigger `sessions_yield` from a main-session conversation; confirm the session entry persists `status: paused` and `pauseReason: sessions_yield`, and that a follow-up turn flips back to `running` with `pauseReason: undefined`.
- [ ] Restart-recovery smoke: kill the gateway while a main session is paused via yield; confirm restart-recovery skips it instead of re-prompting with the interrupted-turn message.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Before this PR, when a top-level (non-subagent) embedded `openclaw` run ended via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner emitted `yielded=true` and `livenessState=paused` but the gateway treated it as a clean completion and wrote `status=done`. The session displayed as finished while awaiting a queued post-yield continuation, racing dashboards, restart-recovery, and follow-up turns. After this PR, the lifecycle persistence derives `status=paused` with `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, restart-recovery skips paused sessions, and `SessionRunStatus` adds `"paused"` so consumers can distinguish a yielded run from a completed one.
- **Real environment tested**: live OpenClaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 65734, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 with this PR cherry-picked). The deployed `dist/server-chat-*.js` and `dist/main-session-restart-recovery-*.js` chunks carry the new `pauseReason`/`sessions_yield` logic; the proof script imports and executes those exact bundle bytes.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # On rh-bot.lan, confirm deployed bundles carry the PR logic:
  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  # -> 1 (the new shouldSkipMainRecovery branch)

  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  # -> non-zero (new deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot branches)

  # Drive the deployed code with three scenarios:
  node /tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs
  ```
  Proof script (`/tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs`) extracts the deployed
  `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` from `server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js` via
  `vm.runInContext`, dynamically imports `main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js`
  to grab the exported `markRestartAbortedMainSessions`, seeds a temp
  state-dir with one yielded and one non-yielded `running` entry, and asserts
  three behaviors:
  1. Lifecycle `phase=end` with `yielded=true` -> `status=paused`,
     `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, `abortedLastRun=false`.
  2. Same entry passed to restart-recovery is SKIPPED (no `abortedLastRun`
     write, `updatedAt` unchanged).
  3. Sibling running entry without `pauseReason` is marked
     `abortedLastRun=true` (existing behavior preserved).
  Plus a guard check: lifecycle `phase=error` with `yielded=true` returns
  `status=failed` with NO `pauseReason` leak.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  === PR #75662 real-behavior proof ===
  host=rh-bot.lan pid=84884 node=v25.8.2
  dist=/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist

  [1] lifecycle bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  [1a] yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"paused","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false,"pauseReason":"sessions_yield"}
  [1a] OK: paused status carries pauseReason=sessions_yield
  [1b] non-yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"done","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1b] OK: non-yielded run preserves status=done with no pauseReason
  [1c] error+yielded -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"failed","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1c] OK: error phase masks yielded marker, no pauseReason leak

  [2] seeded /var/folders/cn/.../state/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  [2] recovery bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  [2] exports: i, n, r, t
  [main-session-restart-recovery] marked 1 interrupted main session(s) for restart recovery (pr-75662-proof)
  [2] mark result: {"marked":1,"skipped":1}
  [2] yielded entry after: {"sessionId":"sess-yielded","status":"running","pauseReason":"sessions_yield","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285519789}
  [2] normal entry after:  {"sessionId":"sess-normal","status":"running","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285521266,"abortedLastRun":true}
  [2a] OK: yielded session was skipped (no abortedLastRun, no updatedAt bump)
  [2b] OK: non-yielded running session was marked abortedLastRun=true

  === ALL PROOFS PASSED ===
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: All three scenarios pass on the deployed bundle. A yielded terminal event produces `status=paused`/`pauseReason=sessions_yield` and is skipped by restart recovery (no `abortedLastRun` mutation, no `updatedAt` bump). A non-yielded terminal event still produces `status=done` with no `pauseReason` (existing behavior preserved). An error phase masks the `yielded` flag and reports `status=failed` with no `pauseReason` leak, matching the gating comment in `resolveTerminalStatus`.
- **Targeted tests**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts` -> 22 passed + 15 passed (37/37). Broader: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts` -> 172/172 passed. `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --threads=4` -> clean.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end live channel trigger (sending a real Telegram message that drives `sessions_yield` to completion); this is exercised by the existing vitest coverage in `pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` rather than reproduced live on rh-bot.

target.title: fix(tools): propagate external content provenance to policy hooks
target.body:
Summary:
- Adds structured external-content provenance to tool-call policy hook types.
- Propagates provenance through HookContext, before_tool_call plugin events, trusted tool policy events, and tool hook context.
- Detects EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT markers from the active embedded run prompt/session context.
- Refreshes provenance after same-run tool results, so fetch/browser/file external content is visible to later policy hooks in the same run when the wrapped tools share the same hook context.
- Documents the public hook field and source semantics.
- Refreshes the plugin SDK type barrel so extension package-boundary cache keys invalidate when hook contract shapes change.

Why:
- External content is already wrapped as untrusted prompt text.
- Before this change, the post-model tool-call policy boundary did not receive structured provenance showing whether external content was present.
- Policy plugins could see the tool name, params, tool kind/input kind, run/toolCallId, derived paths, and session context, but not whether the tool call followed browser, web_fetch, email, webhook, file-fetch, or other untrusted external context.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Same-run external content provenance reaches the later before_tool_call policy boundary after a web_fetch result introduces wrapped EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT. The checked behavior is web_fetch first, then exec later in the same shared HookContext.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw checkout on branch fix/tool-call-external-provenance at PR head e8800a11833801434e6d48a4a0b3f9b03d06d958, running on Linux with Node v24.15.0 and pnpm 11.2.2. The proof used the real active OpenClaw plugin registry, a real trusted tool policy entry, and the real wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook runtime path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
Ran a live `node --import tsx --eval ...` harness in the PR worktree. The harness registered a trusted tool policy in the active plugin registry, wrapped a web_fetch tool and a bash/exec tool with wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook, executed web_fetch first, then executed exec in the same shared hook context.

Evidence after fix:
Terminal capture / copied live output from the real local OpenClaw proof run:

```text
[proof] pr=91800
[proof] head_sha=e8800a11833801434e6d48a4a0b3f9b03d06d958
[proof] using real wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook runtime path
[proof] trusted policy count=1
[proof] shared HookContext initially has externalContent=null
[proof-policy] tool=web_fetch toolCallId=proof-web-fetch-call event.externalContent=null ctx.externalContent=null
[proof-tool] web_fetch returning wrapped external content
[proof-policy] tool=exec toolCallId=proof-exec-call event.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} ctx.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}
[proof-tool] exec would run redacted command
[proof-result] first_call=web_fetch externalContent=null status=PASS
[proof-result] second_call=exec event.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} ctx.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} status=PASS
[proof-result] PASS: same-run external web_fetch content reached later before_tool_call policy boundary through wrapped tool execution
```

Observed result after fix:
Before the web_fetch result returned, the policy hook saw no externalContent. After web_fetch returned wrapped external content with Source: Web Fetch, the later exec policy hook received event.externalContent and ctx.externalContent as {"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}. This proves the same-run provenance signal reaches the later policy boundary after external content is introduced.

What was not tested:
This proof does not claim full browser automation coverage or production split-context run-owned state coverage. It specifically proves the same-run web_fetch-to-exec policy signal path exercised by this PR.

## Validation

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
- pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile
- git diff --check

target.title: feat(feishu): extract option, options, form_value from card action callbacks
target.body:
## Problem

When users interact with Feishu interactive card components like `select_static` (dropdowns), `checkbox` (multi-select), or `form` (form submissions), the selected values are lost because `handleFeishuCardAction` only extracts data from `action.value`.

Feishu sends selection data in separate fields:
- `action.option` — for `select_static` single selection
- `action.options` — for `checkbox` / `multi_select` multiple selections  
- `action.form_value` — for `form` submission data

These fields are completely ignored by the current implementation, making it impossible for AI Agents to know what the user actually selected.

Related issue: #42754

## Solution

1. **Extended `FeishuCardActionEvent` type** to include `option`, `options`, and `form_value` fields
2. **Updated content extraction logic** to merge these fields with `action.value` when present, using priority: `form_value` > `options` > `option` > existing behavior
3. **Maintained full backward compatibility** — existing button interactions (`value.text`, `value.command`) work exactly as before

## Changes

- `extensions/feishu/src/card-action.ts` — Type definition + extraction logic (14 lines changed)
- `extensions/feishu/src/bot.card-action.test.ts` — 4 new test cases (90 lines added)

## Test Plan

- [x] `select_static` with `option` field — value merged into JSON output
- [x] `checkbox` with `options` array — values merged into JSON output  
- [x] `form` with `form_value` object — values merged into JSON output
- [x] Existing button with `value.command` — backward compatible, unchanged behavior
- [x] All 6 tests pass (`vitest`)
- [x] TypeScript compilation passes

## AI Disclosure

This PR was authored with assistance from an AI coding assistant (Claude/Cursor). All code changes have been reviewed and tested.

target.title: Node run/install: add --header for WebSocket upgrade (Cloudflare Zero Trust)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw node run` and `node install` had no way to pass custom HTTP headers on the WebSocket upgrade request, causing silent 302 redirects and connection failures when the node gateway sits behind Cloudflare Zero Trust or similar auth proxies
- **Why it matters:** Teams deploying OpenClaw in Zero Trust network environments (common in enterprise) were completely blocked — the WS handshake would redirect instead of upgrading, with no workaround
- **What changed:** Added a repeatable `--header "Name: Value"` CLI flag to both `node run` and `node install`; headers are persisted to config and forwarded to the gateway WebSocket client; also supports `OPENCLAW_NODE_HEADERS`, `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID`, and `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET` env vars for the installed daemon service
- **What did NOT change:** No changes to gateway routing logic, auth token handling, skill execution, or any behavior for users not passing `--header`

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #42792
- Related: none

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `openclaw node run` now accepts repeatable `--header "Name: Value"` flags
- `openclaw node install` now accepts repeatable `--header "Name: Value"` flags; headers are written to config and used by the installed daemon on startup
- New env vars supported: `OPENCLAW_NODE_HEADERS` (JSON), `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID`, `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET`
- No behavior change for users who do not pass `--header`

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `Yes` — headers may contain auth tokens; persisted to existing node config file (same location/permissions as all other config)
- New/changed network calls? `Yes` — user-supplied headers forwarded on outbound WebSocket upgrade request
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- **Risk + mitigation:** Headers containing secrets are written to the existing node config file (not a new location). No headers are logged or surfaced in output. The `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET` env var name makes sensitivity clear to users.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15.x (Apple Silicon)
- Runtime/container: Node.js v22, pnpm monorepo
- Model/provider: N/A (gateway connectivity issue, not model-specific)
- Integration/channel: Cloudflare Zero Trust (simulated via mock HTTP proxy)
- Relevant config: none required

### Steps

**1. Start a mock proxy that returns 302 if the CF header is absent:**
```js
const http = require('http');
const server = http.createServer((req, res) => {
  if (!req.headers['cf-access-client-id']) {
    res.writeHead(302, { Location: 'https://example.cloudflareaccess.com' });
    res.end();
    return;
  }
  res.writeHead(200);
  res.end();
});
server.listen(9999, () => console.log('Mock proxy listening on 9999'));
```

**2. Without fix — run in second terminal:**
```bash
pnpm openclaw node run --host localhost --port 9999
```

**3. With fix — run with headers:**
```bash
pnpm openclaw node run --host localhost --port 9999 \
  --header "CF-Access-Client-Id: test123" \
  --header "CF-Access-Client-Secret: secret456"
```

### Expected

- WS upgrade request includes supplied headers
- No 302 redirect when correct Zero Trust headers are present

### Actual (before fix)

- WS upgrade sent with no custom headers → Cloudflare returns 302 → `Unexpected server response: 302`

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

**Before fix:**
```
Unexpected server response: 302
```

**After fix:**
```
Unexpected server response: 200
```
_(200 is expected — mock is not a real WS server; the point is the 302 redirect is gone)_

New unit tests added:
- `src/gateway/client.test.ts` — headers passed to WS constructor
- `src/daemon/program-args.test.ts` — `--header` args emitted correctly
- `src/cli/node-cli/header-args.test.ts` — parse logic including edge cases

All 895 test files, 7301 tests passing. `pnpm check` clean.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:**
  - `node run` with single `--header` → header present in WS upgrade
  - `node run` with multiple `--header` flags → all headers forwarded
  - `node run` with no `--header` → behavior identical to pre-fix
  - Mock proxy 302→200 transition confirmed end-to-end
  - `pnpm check` and `pnpm test` both clean on branch
- **Edge cases checked:**
  - Malformed header string (missing `:`) → `parseHeaderArgs` throws with clear error
  - Header value containing `:` (e.g. URLs) → splits on first colon only, value preserved
  - Empty header value → accepted (valid HTTP)
- **What I did not verify:**
  - Live Cloudflare Zero Trust environment (tested via mock proxy only)
  - `node install` daemon persistence across system restarts
  - Windows path behavior for config persistence

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — `--header` is purely additive; existing behavior unchanged
- Config/env changes? `Yes` — new optional `headers` field in node config; old configs without it continue to work
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert: remove `--header` flags from invocation, or delete `headers` key from node config file
- Files/config to restore: `~/.openclaw/node-config.json` — remove the `headers` field
- Known bad symptoms: WS connections failing after upgrade if headers are malformed; `parseHeaderArgs` throws at startup with a descriptive error

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** User accidentally persists a sensitive token to disk via `node install --header`
  - **Mitigation:** Consistent with how other sensitive config (API keys) is already handled in node config. No new attack surface; file permissions unchanged.
- **Risk:** Header injection via malformed `--header` value
  - **Mitigation:** `parseHeaderArgs` validates format; `ws` library handles header serialization — no raw string concatenation into HTTP stream.

target.title: fix(skills): list omitted skill names when prompt is truncated
target.body:
## Problem

When many skills are installed (e.g. 45+), the prompt truncation at `maxSkillsPromptChars` (default 30KB) silently drops skills near the end of the alphabetically-sorted list. The agent sees only a generic `⚠️ Skills truncated: included N of M` message but has **no way to discover which skills were omitted** — it cannot use them even when directly relevant to the user's request.

Real-world example: a user sends a `mp.weixin.qq.com` link, but the `wechat-reader` skill (w-prefix, sorted last) is truncated out. The agent falls back to `curl`, which fails because WeChat articles are JS-rendered.

## Solution

When skills are truncated, the note now includes the **names** of omitted skills (capped at 1KB to stay compact) and suggests `openclaw skills info <name>` or `openclaw skills list` so the agent can still discover and read them on demand.

**Before:**
```
⚠️ Skills truncated: included 40 of 45. Run `openclaw skills check` to audit.
```

**After:**
```
⚠️ Skills truncated: included 40 of 45. Run `openclaw skills check` to audit. Additional installed skills not expanded here: verify-on-browser, wechat-reader, web-search-plus, wrangler, video-frames. If a listed skill name becomes relevant, inspect it with `openclaw skills info <name>` or `openclaw skills list` before deciding whether to read its SKILL.md.
```

## Changes

- `applySkillsPromptLimits()` now returns `omittedSkills` alongside the existing fields
- New `formatTruncatedSkillNames()` builds a compact name list (hard-capped at 1KB)
- New `buildSkillsTruncationNote()` assembles the enriched truncation message
- Two new test cases covering name listing for both char-based and count-based truncation

## Tests

All 34 existing + new tests pass (`npx vitest run src/agents/skills`).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Skills prompt truncation should keep the emitted prompt within `maxSkillsPromptChars` and advertise omitted skill identifiers that `openclaw skills info <name>` can resolve exactly, including raw keys with characters such as `<` and backticks.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Darwin arm64, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 10.33.2, branch `fix/skills-truncation-visibility`, current HEAD `db926508c4`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rendered 80 synthetic workspace skills through the real `buildWorkspaceSkillsPrompt()` code path with `maxSkillsPromptChars: 520` and raw key `skill-00<zero`raw`; then parsed the emitted `<omitted_skill_keys>` payload and resolved the first advertised key through `formatSkillInfo()` exact lookup. For comparison, the same rendering script was also run against local worktree commit `79acf3289d` before the budgeted lookup-key note.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output copied from the local command run.

```text
Before (79acf3289d):
{
  "commit": "before",
  "cap": 520,
  "length": 989,
  "hasOmittedSkillKeys": false,
  "advertisedCount": 0,
  "advertisedFirst": null,
  "hasRawKey": false
}
--- prompt excerpt ---
⚠️ Skills truncated: included 0 of 80 (compact format, descriptions omitted). Omitted: skill-00zeroraw, skill-01, skill-02, skill-03, skill-04, skill-05, skill-06, skill-07, skill-08, skill-09, skill-10, skill-11, skill-12, skill-13, skill-14, skill-15, skill-16, skill-17, skill-18, skill-19, skill-20, skill-21, skill-22, skill-23, skill-24, skill-25, skill-26, skill-27, skill-28, skill-29, skill-30, skill-31, skill-32, skill-33, skill-34, skill-35, skill-36, skill-37, skill-38, skill-39, skill-40, skill-41, skill-42, skill-43, skill-44, skill-45, skill-46, skill-47, skill-48, skill-49, skill-50, skill-51, skill-52, skill-53, skill-54, skill-55, skill-56, skill-57, skill-58, skill-59, skill-60, skill-61, skill-62, skill-63, skill-64, skill-65, skill-66, skill-67, skill-68, skill-69, skill-70, skill-71, skill-72, skill-73, skill-74, skill-75, skill-76, skill-77, skill-78, skill-79. Use `openclaw skills info <name>` to load them on demand. Run `openclaw skills check` to audit.

After (db926508c4):
{
  "commit": "after",
  "cap": 520,
  "length": 512,
  "hasOmittedSkillKeys": true,
  "advertisedCount": 21,
  "advertisedFirst": "skill-00<zero`raw",
  "hasRawKey": true,
  "exactLookupFirst": "skill-00<zero`raw"
}
--- prompt excerpt ---
⚠️ Skills truncated: included 0 of 80 (compact format, descriptions omitted). Omitted skill lookup keys for `openclaw skills info <name>` (JSON data; use exact string values): <omitted_skill_keys>["skill-00\u003czero`raw","skill-01","skill-02","skill-03","skill-04","skill-05","skill-06","skill-07","skill-08","skill-09","skill-10","skill-11","skill-12","skill-13","skill-14","skill-15","skill-16","skill-17","skill-18","skill-19","skill-20"]</omitted_skill_keys> (+59 more) Run `openclaw skills check` to audit.
```

Observed result after fix: The current prompt is 512 chars, below the 520 char cap, includes `<omitted_skill_keys>`, preserves the raw advertised key as JSON data, and `formatSkillInfo()` resolves the first advertised key exactly as `skill-00<zero`raw`.

What was not tested: Live model submission was not needed for this prompt-rendering fix; the local proof covers the real prompt builder and CLI exact lookup formatter path.

Local script generated 80 workspace skills with `maxSkillsPromptChars: 520`, injected the raw omitted lookup key `skill-00<zero`raw`, and rendered the same prompt path through `buildWorkspaceSkillsPrompt()`. The after case also passes the first advertised key through `formatSkillInfo()` exact lookup.

Before (`79acf3289d`, pre budgeted lookup-key note):

Use `openclaw skills info <name>` to load them on demand. Run `openclaw skills check` to audit.

After (`db926508c4`):


target.title: fix #73837: stop after failed Node package installs
target.body:
Fixes #73837.

## Summary
- Stop Linux Node.js installer paths immediately when required package-manager steps fail.
- Keep the existing noninteractive apt wrapper and final Node runtime validation, but prevent failed NodeSource setup/install commands from falling through to a misleading success path.
- Add regression coverage for NodeSource setup failure and apt nodejs install failure when `install_node` is called from a conditional shell context.

## Root Cause
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause Linux installer failure handling bug fix.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High. The patch stays inside `scripts/install.sh` Node runtime installation and the adjacent installer test file.
- **Root cause:** The root cause is Bash's conditional-function `errexit` suppression: because `install_node` can be invoked from an `if install_node` shell context, bare `run_quiet_step` failures inside that function no longer abort the function, which caused failed NodeSource setup or package install commands to continue into later installer logic instead of stopping at the failed step.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** Required Linux Node installation commands now go through `run_required_step`, which exits immediately if `run_quiet_step` reports failure. The existing post-install `finish_linux_node_install` validation remains in place, so success is only printed after a supported `node` binary is active on PATH.
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch quality/default-return warning is intentional: `run_required_step` returns only after a successful wrapped command and exits on failure, so it cannot mask the failed package-manager command. No package manager selection, apt noninteractive flags, Node minimum version, npm install path, or macOS behavior changed.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A failed NodeSource setup or apt `nodejs` install no longer falls through and reports a successful Node.js install before later crashing.
- **Real environment tested:** Disposable Debian 12 Docker container from the local `pgvector/pgvector:pg17` image, with the patched worktree mounted read-only and Docker run with `--network none`. The proof sources production `scripts/install.sh`, sets `OS=linux`, and invokes `install_node` from an `if install_node; then` conditional shell context. The container image has Debian's real `/usr/lib/apt/apt-helper download-file` but no curl/wget, so the proof exposes apt-helper through a temporary curl-compatible wrapper in `/tmp/proof-bin`; no repository code or installer function was changed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-73837
sudo docker run --rm --network none --entrypoint bash \
  -v "$PWD:/repo:ro" -w /repo pgvector/pgvector:pg17 -lc '
    set -Eeuo pipefail
    mkdir -p /tmp/proof-bin
    # /tmp/proof-bin/curl adapts Debian apt-helper download-file to the curl -o URL shape used by install.sh.
    export PATH="/tmp/proof-bin:$PATH"
    export OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1 TERM=dumb CI=1
    source scripts/install.sh
    OS=linux
    if install_node; then echo "UNEXPECTED: install_node returned success"; fi
  '
```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
inside-container: invoking install_node
· Installing Node.js via NodeSource
· Downloading NodeSource setup script
✗ Downloading NodeSource setup script failed — re-run with --verbose for details
Ign:1 https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_24.x
Err:1 https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_24.x
  Temporary failure resolving 'deb.nodesource.com'
E: Failed to fetch https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_24.x  Temporary failure resolving 'deb.nodesource.com'
E: Download Failed
docker_run_exit_status=1
assert:exit_status_1=true
assert:nodesource_step_failed=true
assert:real_dns_failure=true
assert:no_install_node_success=true
assert:no_linux_success_message=true
ASSERT_PASS: install_node exited at the failed required NodeSource step and did not print success
```
- **Observed result after fix:** The production installer reached the required NodeSource download step in a real Linux container, hit the real container DNS/network failure for `deb.nodesource.com`, exited with status 1 through `run_required_step`, and did not continue to `apt_get_install nodejs`, `finish_linux_node_install`, or any Node.js success message.
- **Secondary deterministic regression proof:** I also ran `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-73837-evidence/local-installer-node-failure-proof.mjs`, which sources production `scripts/install.sh` and locks both previously reported conditional-`errexit` failure shapes: NodeSource setup failure and apt `nodejs` install failure both exit with status 1 and never return `install_node` success.
- **What was not tested:** No successful Node installation was performed, and the host package manager was not modified. The real-environment proof intentionally uses a network-disabled disposable container to force the NodeSource failure path; the successful package-manager path remains covered by the existing post-install Node runtime validation rather than by this failure proof.

## Review findings addressed
- **RF-001 — fixed:** Added redacted terminal output from a disposable Debian 12 Docker container where patched production `install_node` hits a real NodeSource DNS/network failure for `https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_24.x`, exits with status 1 at `Downloading NodeSource setup script`, and does not print a Node.js success message.
- **RF-002 — stale / CI infrastructure:** The previously in-progress check runs reached terminal state. Current non-success checks on head `3952d599015d7356ffd5a4251f3255c079141e5f` are self-hosted runner communication failures, with GitHub annotations saying the runner lost communication with the server; there is no current-head test assertion failure attributable to this PR.
- **RF-003 — fixed:** Replaced the prior proof-only fixture story with the real Linux container proof above. The local fixture proof is now only secondary deterministic regression evidence, not the primary real behavior proof.
- **RF-004 — maintainer decision disclosed:** The 21 required Linux Node package-manager setup/install call sites intentionally fail closed on nonzero exit. This is the requested behavior boundary: a failed required Node runtime install should not rely on later validation or fallback before stopping.
- **RF-005 — fixed:** Added actual Linux/container failure output from a disposable Debian container, including the real `Temporary failure resolving 'deb.nodesource.com'` NodeSource failure and the `ASSERT_PASS` checks for exit status and absence of success output.
- **RF-006 — stale / CI infrastructure:** Final CI state is no longer pending; the remaining current-head failures are runner infrastructure failures rather than PR-code failures, so no code change is made for CI.
- **RF-007 — fixed / maintainer review remains:** No automated repair was needed. The contributor-side proof gap is addressed with container evidence; maintainer review of the intentional fail-closed installer semantics still remains appropriate.
- **Proof judgment note:** The local OpenClaw chat/RPC/gateway proof path is not applicable to this reviewer request because the changed behavior is the installer’s Linux package-manager failure path. The applicable local proof path is the Docker/Linux installer run above.

## Tests
- **Target test file:** `test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** NodeSource repository setup failure and apt `nodejs` install failure stop `install_node` immediately and do not print Node.js installation success.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The tests source the real installer script and call `install_node` under the same conditional-shell shape that can suppress `errexit`, while keeping package-manager operations local and deterministic.
- **Exact commands run:**
```bash
PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-73837 exec vitest run test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts --reporter=dot
PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-73837 exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/install.sh test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts
PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-73837 exec oxlint --deny-warnings test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts
OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-73837 check:changed
git -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-73837 diff --check
```
- **Observed test result after fix:** Installer Vitest passed 47/47 tests; `oxfmt --check` passed; `oxlint --deny-warnings` passed; `check:changed` passed the tooling lane; `git diff --check` passed with no output.

## Risk / Scope
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Linux users no longer see a successful Node.js install message after a failed NodeSource setup or apt install step.
- **What did NOT change:** Only required Linux Node package-manager failure handling changed; the Node version floor, NodeSource URL, apt noninteractive wrapper, package-manager detection order, npm installation behavior, Homebrew path, and installer UI copy are unchanged, with no unrelated installer scope expansion.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth boundary is the installer step wrapper plus the existing Node runtime validation path: `run_required_step` owns required command failure handling, and `finish_linux_node_install` remains the validation gate before any installed-success message.
- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: 🚨 availability`, `merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility`.
- **Risk explanation:** The installer now exits earlier when package-manager setup/install commands fail; this changes only failed installation flows that previously continued misleadingly.
- **Why acceptable:** Continuing after a failed required Node runtime install cannot produce a valid OpenClaw install, and the final supported-node validation remains unchanged for successful installs.

target.title: feat(control-ui): add cron calendar timeline view
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a 24h timeline strip to the Cron Jobs page in the Control UI, providing an at-a-glance view of today's scheduled tasks.

Closes #40644

## Screenshots

### Dark Theme — Full View
![Full page dark](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/dark-full.jpg)
*Summary strip with always-on chip, 24h timeline with NOW indicator, cluster badges, color-coded dots*

### Cluster Hover — Overlapping Jobs
![Cluster hover](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/cluster-hover.jpg)
*Hovering the ④ cluster at 22:00 shows 4 overlapping jobs with color-coded dots and times*

### Single Dot Hover — Job Info Popup
![Dot hover](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/dot-hover.jpg)
*Hovering oss-scan dot shows schedule, status, duration, and "Click → view run history" hint*

### Light Theme
![Light theme](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xvchris/cron-calendar-screenshots/main/light-theme.jpg)
*Full light theme support via existing CSS variables*

## Features

### Timeline Strip
- **24-hour timeline track** with grid lines and time labels
- **NOW indicator** — red vertical line showing current time, auto-updates every 60s
- **Color-coded dots** — each job gets a distinct color; green for past (ok), blue pulse for future (pending)
- **Cluster badges** — nearby dots (within 2.5%) auto-merge into a numbered badge; hover to see the full list
- **Hover popups** — JS-driven mouseenter/mouseleave with 150ms debounce for reliable hover behavior
- **Click navigation** — past dots jump to Run History (filtered), future dots scroll to job details
- **Zoom controls** — 24h (full day), Work (08:00–20:00), Now±3h (centered on current time)

### Always-On Chips
- High-frequency jobs (every <10min or cron `*/N` where N≤10) are displayed as chips in the summary strip instead of cluttering the timeline
- Hover to see: View Details / View History / Run Now actions
- Click to jump to run history

### Other
- **Mobile fallback** — timeline hidden on narrow screens, replaced by a simple text list of upcoming jobs
- **Full i18n** — all strings use `t()`, translations added for en, zh-CN, zh-TW, de, es, pt-BR
- **Theme support** — uses only existing CSS variables, works in both dark and light modes
- **No existing features removed** — purely additive changes to the view layer

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `ui/src/ui/helpers/cron-timeline.ts` | **New** — Timeline computation (zoom, clustering, high-freq detection) |
| `ui/src/ui/views/cron.ts` | Added timeline rendering between summary and workspace |
| `ui/src/styles/components.css` | Added timeline CSS (523 lines) |
| `ui/src/i18n/locales/*.ts` | Added `cron.timeline.*` keys to all 6 locales |

## Testing

- Computed timeline data from existing `CronProps` — no controller changes needed
- All new code is additive; existing view functions untouched
- TypeScript strict mode compatible

target.title: fix(web_fetch): strip whitespace from URLs to prevent new URL() parse errors
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91651: LLMs may generate `web_fetch` URLs with accidental whitespace after the scheme (e.g. `"https:// docs.example.com/page"`) which causes `new URL()` to throw. The fix recovers only scheme-adjacent whitespace while preserving valid URL percent-encoding.

**Approach**: Try `new URL(params.url)` first → on failure, strip only whitespace immediately after `http://` or `https://` scheme → if that also fails, reject. Structurally invalid URLs (e.g. hostname-internal whitespace) remain rejected.

## Linked context

Closes #91651

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed:** web_fetch tool rejects URLs with accidental scheme-adjacent whitespace (LLM artifact). Narrow recovery preserves valid URLs and rejects other malformations.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS, Node v22.22.3, latest upstream/main
- **Exact steps or command run:**

```bash
npx tsx -e "
import { createWebFetchTool } from \"./src/agents/tools/web-fetch.js\";
import { createBaseWebFetchToolConfig } from \"./src/agents/tools/web-fetch.test-harness.js\";
import { vi } from \"vitest\";

const lookupMock = vi.fn().mockImplementation(async () => [{ address: \"93.184.216.34\", family: 4 }]);
const baseConfig = createBaseWebFetchToolConfig({ lookupFn: lookupMock });

const fetchSpy = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ ok: true, status: 200, headers: { get: () => \"text/html\" }, text: async () => \"<html>ok</html>\" });
global.fetch = fetchSpy;

const tool = createWebFetchTool(baseConfig);

// Case 1: Accidental space after scheme → recovered ✅
const r1 = await tool?.execute?.(\"call\", { url: \"https:// docs.example.com/page\" });
console.log(\"Case 1 recovered:\", fetchSpy.mock.calls[0]?.[0] === \"https://docs.example.com/page\");

// Case 2: Valid path spaces → preserved as %20 ✅
const r2 = await tool?.execute?.(\"call\", { url: \"https://example.com/a b\" });
console.log(\"Case 2 preserved:\", fetchSpy.mock.calls[1]?.[0]?.includes(\"a%20b\"));

// Case 3: Hostname-internal whitespace → rejected ✅
try { await tool?.execute?.(\"call\", { url: \"https://exa mple.com\" }); console.log(false); } catch { console.log(\"Case 3 rejected: true\"); }
"
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```
Case 1 recovered: true
Case 2 preserved: true
Case 3 rejected: true
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Scheme-adjacent whitespace recovered, valid URLs with path/query spaces preserved as %20, structurally invalid hostnames rejected.
- **What was not tested:** All possible malformed URL combinations. The narrow recovery targets only the reported LLM scheme-space typo.

## Tests and validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/web-fetch.cf-markdown.test.ts --reporter=verbose
# 10 passed (1 test file)
```

- `"recovers from URLs with accidental internal whitespace (#91651)"`
- `"preserves valid path/query spaces as percent-encoding"`
- `"rejects hostname-internal whitespace that is not scheme-adjacent"`

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — scheme-adjacent whitespace URLs now succeed instead of throwing.
**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** No
**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** No
**What is the highest-risk area?** Narrow recovery ensures only scheme-adjacent space is fixed; SSRF guard still validates the parsed URL.
**How is that risk mitigated?** Protocol check + SSRF guard remain after URL parsing.

## Current review state

**What is the next action?** Maintainer review.
**What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?** None.

target.title: [AI-assisted] Fix isolated cron sessions_yield media wait abort
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Isolated cron media runs can call `image_generate`, then correctly call `sessions_yield`, but the attempt still enters the completion-required media wait with the already-aborted internal `sessions_yield` signal and turns the intentional pause into `AbortError: aborted`. The generated-media callback path could also bypass the requester-agent continuation for inactive isolated cron run sessions.
- Solution: Skip the synchronous completion-required media wait only after the attempt has positively detected the intentional `sessions_yield` abort, and keep agent-mediated generated-media completions routed through the requester-agent before any direct media fallback.
- What changed: Added `shouldWaitForCompletionRequiredAsyncTasks`, wired it into embedded attempt finalization, and tightened inactive isolated cron generated-media delivery so `image_generate` completions run the same cron run session first. Regression coverage now proves the wait policy, the clean yield pause, the completion callback, the same-session requester-agent handoff, a `sessions_send` follow-up action with generated media, and the fallback path when requester-agent media evidence is missing.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No provider, channel plugin, config shape, task-registry, dependency, lockfile, migration, auth, or storage behavior changed. Direct generated-media delivery remains as a fallback when the requester-agent does not prove media delivery.

## Motivation

Isolated cron workflows that generate media as an intermediate step should remain resumable after `sessions_yield`. The media callback path already exists; this patch prevents the paused attempt from immediately reusing its own `sessions_yield` abort signal and prevents inactive isolated cron media callbacks from skipping the requester-agent continuation step.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #92120
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: A cron-run `sessions_yield` pause with an active generated-media task no longer enters the completion-required media wait using the already-aborted `sessions_yield` signal. The generated-media callback now enters the same inactive isolated cron run via the requester-agent first, where a follow-up `sessions_send` action can deliver the generated media. Normal non-yield cron media waits and direct generated-media fallback behavior remain covered.
- Real environment tested: Fresh disposable Linux OpenClaw checkouts; before/after diagnostic base `main` at `c692fabeba`; final pushed PR branch commit `5857bb7f3ae6fee4843b327bbf8833e475287e80`; Node `v24.16.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm tsgo:core
node --import tsx tmp-92120-real-image-proof.ts
OPENCLAW_PROOF_ARTIFACT_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-92120-proof-artifacts node --import tsx tmp/proof-92120-cron-media.ts
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts -t "inactive isolated cron media"
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.async-tasks.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts src/agents/agent-run-terminal-outcome.test.ts
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/image-generate-background.test.ts -t "queues a completion event through the shared generated-media wake path"
git diff --check
pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.async-tasks.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.async-tasks.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
final pushed PR branch commit=5857bb7f3ae6fee4843b327bbf8833e475287e80
PROOF_REQUIRES_WITHOUT_YIELD=true
PROOF_HAS_YIELD_POLICY=true
PROOF_SHOULD_WAIT_AFTER_YIELD=false
PROOF_OUTCOME=patched_paused_no_wait

REAL_IMAGE_PROOF=PASS
IMAGE_FILE=generated-daily.png
IMAGE_EXISTS=true
IMAGE_BYTES=225
IMAGE_SHA256=3a23e2ad20572f384a5e7028862556e8c2f70c2a98a57ccefff83a4cb43565c1
CRON_RUN_SESSION=agent:main:cron:daily-media:run:run-real-image-proof
CALLBACK_SOURCE_TOOL=image_generate
CALLBACK_SOURCE_SESSION=image_generate:task-real-image-proof
REQUESTER_AGENT_CALLS=1
REQUESTER_AGENT_SESSION_KEY=agent:main:cron:daily-media:run:run-real-image-proof
FOLLOWUP_TOOL=sessions_send
FOLLOWUP_MEDIA_MATCH=true
DIRECT_FALLBACK_SENDS=0
DELIVERY_RESULT=delivered:true,path:direct
EXTERNAL_PROVIDER_USED=false

ACTUAL_GATEWAY_CRON_RUN=PASS
CRON_RPC_RUN_FORCE=PASS
JOB_SESSION_TARGET=isolated
MODEL_TOOL_SEQUENCE=image_generate,sessions_yield,sessions_send,final,final
IMAGE_PROVIDER_REQUESTS=1
CALLBACK_RESUME_SEEN=true
CALLBACK_INPUT_HAS_SOURCE_TOOL_IMAGE_GENERATE=true
SESSIONS_SEND_CALLED=true
NESTED_MAIN_FINAL_SEEN=true
DIRECT_FALLBACK_IMAGE_EVENTS=0
OPENCLAW_SAVED_IMAGE_BYTES=702
OPENCLAW_SAVED_IMAGE_SHA256=94eff542b057e38dada70a97fe6a1eb5d1737318a519eb545da0ed6a2eb4ce85

pnpm tsgo:core PASS
inactive isolated cron media focused callback/follow-up test: 4/4 PASS, 192 skipped
subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts: 98/98 PASS
attempt.async-tasks.test.ts: 8/8 PASS
agent-run-terminal-outcome.test.ts: 12/12 PASS
sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts: 4/4 PASS
image-generate-background focused wake-path test: 2/2 PASS, 6 skipped
git diff --check PASS
oxfmt changed files PASS on 5 files
```

- Observed result after fix: The paused attempt no longer waits on a `sessions_yield`-aborted signal. The yielded run exits as clean `end_turn` and is no longer active. The real image proof created an actual PNG (`generated-daily.png`, 225 bytes, SHA-256 `3a23e2ad20572f384a5e7028862556e8c2f70c2a98a57ccefff83a4cb43565c1`) and passed that media artifact through the inactive isolated cron `image_generate` completion callback into the same requester-agent run session. The requester-agent then emitted `sessions_send` with matching media evidence, and direct fallback sends stayed at `0`. A separate real Gateway/Cron proof started OpenClaw's Gateway server, added and force-ran an isolated cron job through `GatewayClient`, invoked `image_generate`, paused with `sessions_yield`, resumed from the generated-media callback with `sourceTool=image_generate`, called `sessions_send`, reached the main session follow-up, saved the generated PNG through OpenClaw media storage, and recorded `DIRECT_FALLBACK_IMAGE_EVENTS=0`.
- What was not tested: No live external image provider credential, real Google/OpenAI image generation call, full repository-wide suite, browser/UI path, production channel delivery, or real user account behavior was used. The image proof used deterministic/local provider-controlled PNG artifacts so the runner/session/media-delivery layer could be verified without provider flake.
- Before evidence:

```text
unpatched main commit=c692fabeba
PROOF_REQUIRES_WITHOUT_YIELD=true
PROOF_HAS_YIELD_POLICY=false
PROOF_SHOULD_WAIT_AFTER_YIELD=true
PROOF_OUTCOME=base_abort_error
PROOF_ERROR_NAME=AbortError
PROOF_ERROR_MESSAGE=aborted
PROOF_ERROR_CAUSE=sessions_yield
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `sessions_yield` intentionally aborts the internal attempt controller with reason `sessions_yield`, but the post-prompt completion-required media wait still ran after that pause and reused the already-aborted signal. Separately, inactive cron generated-media completions had a stale-run shortcut that could direct-deliver media before the requester-agent continuation ran.
- Missing detection / guardrail: The completion-required media wait policy did not distinguish a clean yield pause from an ordinary cron run that must synchronously wait for active generated-media tasks before finalizing. The inactive isolated cron delivery path also did not distinguish generated-media tools that require agent-mediated continuation from non-agent-mediated stale cron completions.
- Contributing context (if known): Prior cron media work correctly waits for generated-media tasks when no yield path is used. The missing guard was at the handoff between intentional yield cleanup, cron media waiting, and generated-media callback delivery for inactive cron run sessions.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.async-tasks.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts`, `src/agents/agent-run-terminal-outcome.test.ts`, `src/agents/tools/image-generate-background.test.ts`, and `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: A cron run with an active completion-required generated-media task still waits when no yield happened, skips that synchronous wait after a confirmed `sessions_yield` pause, then handles the generated-media completion through the same inactive isolated cron run session before falling back to direct media delivery only if the requester-agent did not deliver the media.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug crosses two local OpenClaw boundaries: the post-yield wait decision and the generated-media completion delivery decision. Testing those boundaries directly avoids provider flake while still proving the requested follow-up action (`sessions_send`) runs from the same cron run session.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing tests covered clean yield exit, generated-media wake events, active requester media completion delivery, and normal cron media wait behavior, but not inactive isolated cron media callback continuation after `sessions_yield`.
- If no new test is added, why not: New regression tests are added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Isolated cron workflows that intentionally yield while waiting for generated-media completion should stay in the resumable completion-event path instead of reporting `AbortError: aborted` immediately after the yield. When the media callback arrives after the cron run is inactive, OpenClaw now gives the same isolated cron run session the first chance to continue and send the follow-up message/action.

## Diagram

```text
image_generate starts generated-media task
  -> sessions_yield intentionally pauses the attempt
  -> post-prompt cron media wait sees yieldDetected=true and does not wait on the aborted signal
  -> generated-media completion callback arrives for image_generate:task-123
  -> inactive isolated cron run is not steered because it is no longer active
  -> requester-agent runs with sessionKey=agent:main:cron:daily-media:run:run-123
  -> requester-agent performs sessions_send follow-up with generated media
  -> direct generated-media fallback remains available only when requester-agent media evidence is missing
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? No.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Not applicable.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux disposable test checkout.
- Runtime/container: Node `v24.16.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`.
- Model/provider: No external provider call; deterministic OpenClaw source/runtime harnesses.
- Integration/channel (if any): Embedded runner cron media wait, sessions-yield orchestration, generated-media completion wake, inactive isolated cron requester-agent delivery, `sessions_send` follow-up evidence, and direct generated-media fallback.
- Relevant config (redacted): `OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1` for focused Vitest runs.

### Steps

1. Reproduce the old decision path on unpatched `main` with an active cron-owned `image_generation` task and `sessions_yield` abort reason.
2. Apply this patch and confirm the same active task still requires a wait without yield but does not require a wait after `yieldDetected=true`.
3. Generate a real local PNG artifact and pass it through the inactive isolated cron `image_generate` completion callback.
4. Prove the callback calls the requester-agent on the same cron run session.
5. Prove the requester-agent follow-up contains `sessions_send` delivery evidence with the generated media.
6. Prove direct generated-media fallback remains only when requester-agent media evidence is missing.
7. Start a real OpenClaw Gateway server, create an isolated cron job through the Gateway RPC client, force-run it, let the model-provider double emit `image_generate`, `sessions_yield`, and `sessions_send`, then verify the generated-media callback resume and saved image artifact.
8. Run focused and adjacent source tests for wait policy, clean yield exit, terminal outcome, image completion wake, inactive cron callback continuation, full announcement delivery, typecheck, whitespace, and formatting.

### Expected

- Non-yield cron media runs keep the synchronous generated-media wait.
- Yielded cron media runs do not reuse the `sessions_yield`-aborted signal for the synchronous wait.
- Yielded sessions still exit as clean `end_turn` runs and are no longer active.
- Generated-media completions for inactive isolated cron runs route through the requester-agent on the same cron run session before direct fallback.
- The requester-agent follow-up can send the generated media through `sessions_send`.
- Direct generated-media fallback stays available when the requester-agent misses media delivery evidence.

### Actual

- Unpatched `main`: the simulated post-yield wait decision entered the wait and threw `AbortError` with cause `sessions_yield`.
- Patched branch: the post-yield wait decision returned false while the non-yield media wait decision still returned true. The inactive isolated cron media callback called the requester-agent on the same cron run session and accepted `sessions_send` media delivery evidence for the generated PNG artifact; the fallback-only test still delivered generated media directly after requester-agent media evidence was missing.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
Before:
PROOF_OUTCOME=base_abort_error
PROOF_ERROR_NAME=AbortError
PROOF_ERROR_CAUSE=sessions_yield

After:
PROOF_OUTCOME=patched_paused_no_wait
REAL_IMAGE_PROOF=PASS
IMAGE_FILE=generated-daily.png
IMAGE_EXISTS=true
IMAGE_BYTES=225
IMAGE_SHA256=3a23e2ad20572f384a5e7028862556e8c2f70c2a98a57ccefff83a4cb43565c1
ACTUAL_GATEWAY_CRON_RUN=PASS
CRON_RPC_RUN_FORCE=PASS
JOB_SESSION_TARGET=isolated
MODEL_TOOL_SEQUENCE=image_generate,sessions_yield,sessions_send,final,final
IMAGE_PROVIDER_REQUESTS=1
CALLBACK_RESUME_SEEN=true
CALLBACK_INPUT_HAS_SOURCE_TOOL_IMAGE_GENERATE=true
SESSIONS_SEND_CALLED=true
NESTED_MAIN_FINAL_SEEN=true
DIRECT_FALLBACK_IMAGE_EVENTS=0
OPENCLAW_SAVED_IMAGE_BYTES=702
OPENCLAW_SAVED_IMAGE_SHA256=94eff542b057e38dada70a97fe6a1eb5d1737318a519eb545da0ed6a2eb4ce85
FOLLOWUP_TOOL=sessions_send
FOLLOWUP_MEDIA_MATCH=true
DIRECT_FALLBACK_SENDS=0
DELIVERY_RESULT=delivered:true,path:direct
pnpm tsgo:core PASS
inactive isolated cron media focused callback/follow-up test: 4/4 PASS, 192 skipped
subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts: 98/98 PASS
attempt.async-tasks.test.ts: 8/8 PASS
agent-run-terminal-outcome.test.ts: 12/12 PASS
sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts: 4/4 PASS
image-generate-background focused wake-path test: 2/2 PASS, 6 skipped
git diff --check PASS
oxfmt changed files PASS on 5 files

Callback/follow-up proof details:
- actual Gateway cron run passed through Gateway RPC add/run
- isolated cron job target was preserved
- model tool sequence was image_generate -> sessions_yield -> sessions_send
- generated-media callback resume included sourceTool=image_generate
- requester agent called once
- sessionKey=agent:main:cron:daily-media:run:run-real-image-proof
- sourceTool=image_generate
- sourceSessionKey=image_generate:task-real-image-proof
- delivery evidence tool=sessions_send
- delivery evidence media matches generated-daily.png
- direct fallback sendMessage was not called when requester-agent media evidence existed
- direct fallback sendMessage was called when requester-agent media evidence was missing
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Base-vs-patched wait decision, non-yield generated-media wait preservation, clean sessions-yield `end_turn` behavior, terminal-outcome coverage, image completion wake path, real generated PNG artifact, real Gateway RPC cron add/run, isolated cron target, `image_generate -> sessions_yield -> callback resume -> sessions_send` model sequence, OpenClaw-saved generated image artifact, inactive isolated cron callback continuation through the same requester-agent session, `sessions_send` follow-up with matching generated media, direct fallback after missing media evidence, full announcement delivery suite, core typecheck, formatting, and whitespace.
- Edge cases checked: `yieldDetected=true` only skips the synchronous wait after the attempt has confirmed the `sessions_yield` abort; ordinary cron media waits still return true for active image-generation tasks; generated-media callbacks use the requester-agent path before direct fallback; non-agent-mediated stale cron completion behavior is left on the existing direct/no-op path.
- What you did **not** verify: Live external image provider generation, production channel delivery, full repository-wide suite, browser/UI behavior, or real user account behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

The latest ClawSweeper review found a missing import and requested a stronger real image continuation proof. The import is fixed in `5857bb7f3ae6fee4843b327bbf8833e475287e80`, and the real Gateway/Cron/PNG continuation proof is included above. No review conversation was resolved by this PR body update.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Not applicable.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: The completion-required media wait could be skipped too broadly.
  - Mitigation: The helper skips only when the attempt has already positively classified the abort as `sessions_yield`; the regression test asserts non-yield cron media tasks still require the wait.
- Risk: Inactive isolated cron completions could skip the requester-agent continuation.
  - Mitigation: Agent-mediated generated-media completions no longer use the stale cron direct shortcut first; the new test proves same-session requester-agent continuation and `sessions_send` delivery evidence.
- Risk: Removing the old direct shortcut for generated-media cron completions could drop media delivery if the requester-agent misses the media.
  - Mitigation: A paired regression test proves direct generated-media fallback still fires after requester-agent media delivery evidence is missing.
- Risk: Live provider behavior could differ from the deterministic source harness.
  - Mitigation: The bug is in local runner/session/delivery control flow after `sessions_yield`; the proof isolates that path without relying on provider availability. No external provider success is claimed.

target.title: [codex] fix gateway hot-mode restart reloads
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a stale runtime-config hole in gateway config reloads: when `gateway.reload.mode` is `hot` and a config file change requires a gateway restart, the reloader now schedules the existing restart path instead of warning and returning.

This prevents doctor/configure or external config writes from leaving the live gateway on stale model/provider/auth runtime state after disk config has been repaired.

## Details

- In hot mode, restart-required reload plans now log `hot mode scheduling restart` and call the existing `onRestart` path.
- Existing idle-aware restart machinery remains responsible for deferring or forcing the process restart.
- Added a regression test for `gateway.auth.token` changes in hot mode so restart-required auth changes cannot silently stay stale.
- Added a regression test for doctor-style OpenAI Codex model route repairs so model route changes still hot-reload when no restart-only path is present.
- Updated gateway reload docs so `hot` mode no longer promises restart-required changes will be ignored.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: in hot reload mode, when a config write touches any restart-required key (e.g. `auth.profiles.*`, `models.pricing`, `gateway.*`, etc.), the gateway's reload runtime dropped the entire plan with a single `log.warn` and returned. The result was the gateway kept running with the previous snapshot in memory while the on-disk config had already been repaired. Observed in production on the deployment fork as a 4-hour silent outage where the in-process agent kept resolving the legacy `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` model id after `openclaw doctor --fix` had migrated the on-disk config to `openai/gpt-5.5`. Group Telegram messages silently dropped while admin DMs returned a canned `[agent failed]` reply that masked the failure.

- **Real environment tested**: deployed `openclaw` gateway on `gateway-host` (macOS, launchd-supervised, KeepAlive=dict { Crashed=true, SuccessfulExit=false }) running `upgrade-v2026.6.2` deployment fork with this PR's commit cherry-picked, build commit `421a97cf2d`. Reload-event capture below via `ssh user@gateway-host` against `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` and `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  ssh user@gateway-host 'cp -p ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak'
  ssh user@gateway-host 'jq ".gateway.reload.mode = \"hot\"" ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json > /tmp/c.json && mv /tmp/c.json ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json'
  ssh user@gateway-host 'jq ".models.pricing = {enabled: true}" ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json > /tmp/c.json && mv /tmp/c.json ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json'
  ssh user@gateway-host 'grep -E "hot mode scheduling restart|hot mode ignoring" ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log | tail -5'
  ssh user@gateway-host 'cp ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json'
  ssh user@gateway-host 'launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/ai.openclaw.gateway'
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**: Captured live from `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log` and `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` on `gateway-host` via `ssh user@gateway-host` against the deployed `openclaw` gateway on commit `421a97cf2d` (PID <gateway-pid> at capture time, restarted to 93522 after the proof).

  ```
  $ grep -E "hot mode (ignoring|scheduling restart)" ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log | tail -5
  2026-04-22T14:18:06.576-04:00 [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode ignoring (plugins.entries.whatsapp.enabled)
  2026-04-24T08:34:56.747-04:00 [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode ignoring (plugins.entries.openai.config, plugins.entries.acpx.config.timeoutSeconds)
  2026-06-02T10:10:59.246-04:00 [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode ignoring (auth.profiles.openai-codex:user@example.com, auth.profiles.openai:user@example.com)
  2026-06-02T18:11:56.785-04:00 [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode scheduling restart (models.pricing)
  ```

  The first three lines are historical pre-fix instances of the silent-staleness mode this PR addresses, including the 2026-06-02T10:10:59Z production incident that motivated the patch (`auth.profiles.openai-codex:...` rename triggered `hot mode ignoring`; the runtime kept the stale `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` model id for 4 hours until `launchctl kickstart -k` was run by hand). The fourth line is the after-fix capture from a controlled hot-mode + restart-required write to `models.pricing.enabled` — the new `hot mode scheduling restart` text appears and the restart path is actually called:

  ```
  $ awk '/2026-06-02T18:11:5[6-7]/' ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log
  2026-06-02T18:11:56.784-04:00 [reload] config change detected; evaluating reload (models.pricing)
  2026-06-02T18:11:56.943-04:00 [gateway] signal SIGUSR1 received
  2026-06-02T18:11:56.943-04:00 [gateway] received SIGUSR1; restarting
  2026-06-02T18:11:56.955-04:00 [shutdown] started: gateway restarting
  2026-06-02T18:11:56.955-04:00 [plugins] memory-lancedb: stopped
  2026-06-02T18:11:57.097-04:00 [gmail-watcher] gmail watcher stopped
  2026-06-02T18:11:57.098-04:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 144ms
  2026-06-02T18:11:57.104-04:00 [gateway] restart mode: full process restart (supervisor restart)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: The patched `hot` branch fires correctly. `[reload]` detects the change, emits the new `hot mode scheduling restart (models.pricing)` warn line, the existing idle-aware restart machinery sends SIGUSR1, the gateway runs a clean shutdown, and `[gateway] restart mode: full process restart (supervisor restart)` records the handoff to the supervisor for respawn. The 4-hour silent staleness failure mode (`hot mode ignoring`) is replaced with a single restart cycle.

  **Honest follow-up finding from this capture**: on `gateway-host` the launchd plist uses `KeepAlive: { Crashed=true, SuccessfulExit=false }` to short-circuit the historical zombie-on-port cascade from the isolated-session SIGKILL path. The patched supervisor-restart hand-off exits with code 0 ("completed cleanly"), and launchd's `SuccessfulExit=false` then refuses to respawn — the gateway stays down until `launchctl kickstart -k` runs externally. This is not a regression introduced by this PR (the old `hot mode ignoring` path also left the gateway stale), but maintainers should know: deployment hosts that use `KeepAlive: { SuccessfulExit: false }` to dodge a different failure cascade need a separate kickstart-on-clean-exit watcher to make this PR's `queueRestart` self-heal end-to-end. The deployment fork ships such a watcher as a follow-up; the patch itself remains correct.

- **What was not tested**: the auto-respawn end-to-end behavior on `KeepAlive=true` hosts (or hosts that wrap the gateway in a non-launchd supervisor) was not driven from this PR; the change was verified by triggering the SIGUSR1 + clean-shutdown sequence and observing the patched warn line and the existing restart-path call. A live Telegram round-trip immediately following the restart was not driven to keep the disruption window short (config was reverted in under 90s; gateway was kickstarted back to PID <gateway-pid-after-restart> with `agent model: openai/gpt-5.5` and `/health` returning 200 as before).



## Operator-side answer to the launchd-respawn gap

The "honest follow-up" section above flagged that hosts using `KeepAlive: { SuccessfulExit: false }` to dodge a separate failure cascade do not respawn the gateway after this PR's clean-exit restart hand-off. To keep the patched gateway self-healing on those hosts, the deployment fork ships a separate LaunchAgent watchdog (`com.openclaw.gateway-respawn-watchdog`) that runs on a 30-second interval, checks both `launchctl print` state AND port-18789 binding, and calls `launchctl kickstart -k` only when both indicate the gateway is fully down. The double-condition gate prevents collisions with the isolated-session SIGKILL respawn cascade that motivated the original `KeepAlive=dict` policy.

Watchdog is independent of this PR's diff (it lives in the deploy-fork host config, not in the openclaw runtime), but documenting it here so the launchd-respawn gap clawsweeper flagged is paired with the production-deploy fix that closes it end-to-end:

```
$ ssh user@gateway-host '/bin/launchctl print "gui/$(id -u)/com.openclaw.gateway-respawn-watchdog" | grep -E "state|run interval"'
        state = not running
        run interval = 30 seconds
                state = active
                state = active

$ ssh user@gateway-host 'cat ~/bin/openclaw-gateway-respawn-watchdog.sh | head -30'
#!/bin/bash
# Respawn watchdog for the openclaw gateway. Runs every 30s via launchd
# StartInterval. If the gateway is "not running" AND nothing is bound to
# port 18789, kickstart it. Both conditions must hold to avoid colliding
# with the isolated-session SIGKILL respawn cascade or with operator-driven
# transient stops.
```

Verified end to end: a controlled hot-mode + restart-required write triggered the new `hot mode scheduling restart (models.pricing)` log line, the gateway's existing supervised-restart machinery logged `restart mode: full process restart (supervisor restart)` and clean-exited, and the watchdog noticed within the 30-second interval and ran `launchctl kickstart -k`. The gateway came back up on a fresh PID in ~6 seconds after the clean exit, with `[gateway] ready` followed by Telegram polling and `/health` returning 200.

Maintainers who do not run `KeepAlive: { SuccessfulExit: false }` will see the launchd-default respawn behavior on the same SIGUSR1 + clean-exit path and do not need the watchdog. Operators who do (because of the isolated-session SIGKILL cascade or a similar concern) get a clean, scoped recovery mechanism that does not require widening `KeepAlive` back to `true`.
