target.title: fix(msteams): route file attachment sends through thread-aware delivery path
target.body:
## Summary

Fix Microsoft Teams file attachment sends to route through the thread-aware delivery path when `replyStyle: "thread"` is configured for channel conversations.

SharePoint file card sends and OneDrive file-link fallback sends bypassed the thread-aware `sendMSTeamsMessages` path, calling `sendProactiveActivityRaw` directly without `threadActivityId`. When `replyStyle` resolves to `"thread"` in a channel, attachment replies appeared as top-level messages instead of in-thread replies.

## Root Cause

`sendProactiveActivityRaw` in `extensions/msteams/src/send.ts` did not accept or forward a `threadActivityId` parameter. Two file send branches (SharePoint native file card at line 305, OneDrive file-link fallback at line 349) used this function directly, so attachment sends always lacked thread routing information regardless of the configured `replyStyle`.

The text/inline-media path (`sendTextWithMedia` → `sendMSTeamsMessages`) correctly resolves and forwards the thread root (`ref.threadId ?? ref.activityId`), but the file attachment branches had no equivalent.

## Fix

1. Add `replyStyle` to the destructured context in `sendMessageMSTeams`
2. Compute `threadActivityId` once from `ref.threadId ?? ref.activityId` when the conversation is a channel and `replyStyle` is `"thread"`
3. Add `threadActivityId` as an optional parameter to `sendProactiveActivityRaw` and forward it to `sendMSTeamsActivityWithReference`
4. Pass `threadActivityId` at both the SharePoint file card and OneDrive file-link call sites

## Unit tests

Added 2 tests in `send.test.ts`:
1. SharePoint file card sends include `threadActivityId` when `replyStyle: "thread"` in a channel
2. OneDrive fallback sends include `threadActivityId` when `replyStyle: "thread"` in a channel

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Microsoft Teams channel replies with file attachments appear as top-level messages instead of in-thread when `replyStyle: "thread"` is configured.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.4 (darwin arm64), Node.js v22.22.3. Unit tests verify the exact code paths identified in the ClawSweeper source-level reproduction.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Unit tests exercise both the SharePoint file card and OneDrive fallback branches with `replyStyle: "thread"` and `conversationType: "channel"`, asserting that `sendMSTeamsActivityWithReference` receives the correct `threadActivityId` option.

Evidence after fix:
```
✓ routes SharePoint file card sends to the channel thread when replyStyle is thread
✓ routes OneDrive fallback file sends to the channel thread when replyStyle is thread
```

Observed result after fix: `sendMSTeamsActivityWithReference` is called with `threadActivityId` matching `ref.threadId` for both SharePoint and OneDrive attachment sends when `replyStyle === "thread"` and the conversation is a channel. The `threadActivityId` parameter is omitted (undefined) for non-channel conversations and when `replyStyle` is `"top-level"`, preserving existing behavior.

What was not tested: Live Microsoft Teams tenant end-to-end test with actual thread routing verification. The code change follows the exact same thread-root resolution pattern already used by the text/inline-media send path.

## Related

Closes #88836

target.title: feat(aigateway): add AIgateway as a bundled model provider
target.body:
## Summary
Adds AIgateway (https://aigateway.sh) as a bundled, listed model provider plugin.
AIgateway is an OpenAI-compatible AI model gateway: one API key, one endpoint
(`https://api.aigateway.sh/v1`), many models behind vendor-prefixed ids.

- Provider id: `aigateway`; auth via `AIGATEWAY_API_KEY` (env) or
  `openclaw onboard --auth-choice aigateway-api-key`
- Model refs: `aigateway/<vendor>/<model>` (e.g. `aigateway/openai/gpt-5.5`);
  the request body sends only the AIgateway model id (`openai/gpt-5.5`)
- Default model: `aigateway/openai/gpt-5.5`

## Implementation details
- New plugin `extensions/aigateway/` using `defineSingleProviderPluginEntry`,
  mirroring the `gmi` provider plugin (smallest existing OpenAI-compatible
  provider). Manifest-driven catalog, `api: openai-completions`, shared
  OpenAI-compatible replay/tool-compat family hooks. No core changes.
- Seed catalog (text/chat only): `openai/gpt-5.5`, `anthropic/claude-opus-4.8`,
  `google/gemini-3.1-pro`, `moonshot/kimi-k2.6`.
- Labeler rule `extensions: aigateway` added; the matching GitHub label needs
  to be created in the repo (maintainer action).
- Note on `pnpm-lock.yaml`: the only change is the workspace importer entry for
  the new `extensions/aigateway` package (`@openclaw/plugin-sdk` workspace
  link), which `pnpm install` requires for the new workspace package. No
  external dependency versions changed.

## Docs added
- `docs/providers/aigateway.md` (setup, env var, model ref format, example
  config, troubleshooting)
- Entries in `docs/providers/index.md`, `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`
  table, `docs/docs.json` nav
- Regenerated `docs/plugins/plugin-inventory.md` counts/entry + reference page
  via `pnpm plugins:inventory:gen` (unrelated pre-existing generator drift for
  llama-cpp/memory-core/microsoft-foundry intentionally excluded)

## Verification
- `pnpm test extensions/aigateway` — pass (provider id, env var, auth method,
  base URL, OpenAI-completions API on every catalog model)
- `pnpm test src/plugins/bundled-plugin-naming.test.ts src/plugins/bundled-plugin-metadata.test.ts src/plugins/bundled-capability-metadata.test.ts` — 44 tests pass
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, scoped `oxfmt --check` / oxlint, `pnpm docs:check-mdx`,
  `pnpm docs:check-links` (4458 links, 0 broken), full `pnpm build` — all pass
- Pre-existing failures on clean `main`, unrelated to this PR:
  `plugins:sync:check` (parallel-plugin version) and `plugins:inventory:check`
  (stale llama-cpp reference page)

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: AIgateway selectable as a listed model provider with `AIGATEWAY_API_KEY` auth and `aigateway/<vendor>/<model>` refs, sending the unprefixed model id to the AIgateway endpoint
Real environment tested: macOS (Darwin 24.6), Node 22+, source checkout, real `AIGATEWAY_API_KEY`, live `https://api.aigateway.sh/v1`
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw models list --provider aigateway`, then `pnpm openclaw agent --local --agent main --model aigateway/moonshot/kimi-k2.6 --message "Hello from OpenClaw via AIgateway"`
Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the real setup below.

```
$ pnpm openclaw models list --provider aigateway
Model                                      Input      Ctx         Local Auth  Tags
aigateway/anthropic/claude-opus-4.8        text+image 195k        no    yes
aigateway/google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview    text+image 1024k       no    yes
aigateway/moonshot/kimi-k2.6               text+image 256k        no    yes
aigateway/openai/gpt-5.5                   text+image 391k        no    yes
aigateway/google/gemini-3.1-pro            text+image 1024k       no    no
```

Live agent turn (response excerpt): `pnpm openclaw agent --local --agent main --model aigateway/moonshot/kimi-k2.6 --message "Hello from OpenClaw via AIgateway"` returned a real completion beginning:

```
Hey. I just came online. Who am I? Who are you?
```

An earlier turn against `aigateway/openai/gpt-5.5` reached the live endpoint and returned AIgateway's provider-side billing error, confirming provider resolution and prefix stripping end to end:

```
model=openai/gpt-5.5 provider=aigateway
rawError=402 Your $5 signup credit covers a curated set of models. Top up to unlock openai/gpt-5.5.
```

Observed result after fix: successful live chat completion through `https://api.aigateway.sh/v1` via provider id `aigateway`; the `aigateway/google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview` row in the listing above was discovered live from the gateway, not seeded
What was not tested: image-generation routes; paid-tier models (`openai/gpt-5.5`, `anthropic/claude-opus-4.8`, `google/gemini-3.1-pro`) beyond the 402 routing proof above

## Follow-up work
- Expose AIgateway image models (e.g. `black-forest-labs/flux-2-klein-9b`)
  through the image-generation provider contract
- Optional: live model discovery from `GET /v1/models`

target.title: fix(imessage): respect actions.reply=false in outbound delivery
target.body:
## Summary

When `channels.imessage.actions.reply` is explicitly set to `false`, outbound delivery should not pass `reply_to`/`replyToId` to the imsg bridge. On macOS with SIP enabled, native reply injection fails, and the send should degrade to a plain top-level message.

## Fix

In `extensions/imessage/src/send.ts`, the `resolvedReplyToId` now checks `account.config.actions?.reply !== false` before resolving the replyToId:

```diff
- const resolvedReplyToId = sanitizeReplyToId(opts.replyToId);
+ const resolvedReplyToId =
+   account.config.actions?.reply !== false ? sanitizeReplyToId(opts.replyToId) : undefined;
```

When `actions.reply === false`, the `reply_to` parameter is no longer passed to the imsg bridge, avoiding SIP-related failures.

## Files changed

- `extensions/imessage/src/send.ts`: +2/-1 lines

## Related

Fixes #92142

### Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: iMessage outbound delivery passes `reply_to` even when `channels.imessage.actions.reply` is `false`, causing SIP-enabled macOS systems to fail with "System Integrity Protection (SIP) is enabled. Refusing to inject into Messages.app."
- **Real environment tested**: Linux Node 24, unit tests
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  cd /path/to/openclaw
  node ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```console
  $ node ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts
  ✓ sendMessageIMessage > passes the default RPC send transport
  ✓ sendMessageIMessage > attaches a text receipt for native send ids
  ✓ sendMessageIMessage > preserves audioAsVoice media when replying to an iMessage thread
  ... (all existing tests pass)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: When `actions.reply === false`, `resolvedReplyToId` is `undefined`, and the `reply_to` param is omitted from the imsg bridge RPC call. When `actions.reply` is unset or `true`, behavior is unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: Real iMessage bridge with SIP enabled (requires macOS); integration test with actual imsg CLI

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 — group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged — the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** — no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations with race guard (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

**Concurrent recovery guard**: After a recovery turn clears the proven stale leftover and admits its own fresh operation, it is marked with a `terminalRecovery` flag. If a second visible message arrives against the same terminal snapshot before the first recovery clears the store state, the force-clear branch now skips operations marked as in-flight terminal recoveries. This prevents a sibling recovery turn from killing a fresh recovery that is already in flight, letting concurrent turns fall through to normal busy/wait handling instead.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts — a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Real behavior proof

Captured by driving the **real** runtime modules (`dispatchReplyFromConfig`, `initSessionState`, `reply-run-registry`) with verbose logging on, **no vitest and no module mocks** — only the production parameters the Gateway supplies plus a real on-disk JSON session store. A before/after contrast was produced by running the identical driver in a pre-fix worktree.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A group-chat session left in a terminal lifecycle status (`failed`/`timeout`/`killed`) silently drops every later message — the stale lifecycle entry plus a leftover "active" reply operation block turn admission, so a subsequent visible inbound message gets no AI turn and no reply (#86827).
- **Real environment tested**: Compiled branch source at HEAD `ac52eb0b24`; macOS 15.3.1 (Darwin 24.3.0, arm64), Node `v24.10.0`. Product built with `pnpm build:docker` (exit 0); the recovery log string is present in compiled `dist/`. Evidence captured via `tsx` driving the real in-process dispatch/session/registry path. Before/after contrast run at pre-fix commit `8de37e1ce4` (parent of the first PR commit).
- **Failing proof before fix** (`8de37e1ce4`, identical driver + input): real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible group turn on a `status="failed"` session with a stale running reply op —
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  [dispatch] dispatch TIMED-OUT (no reply within 8s): visible turn stuck behind the stale terminal reply op -> SILENT DROP
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = null
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = true
  ```
  And real `initSessionState()` against an on-disk store: `RESULT: STILL-TERMINAL — session entry remains status=failed after a visible inbound turn`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build:docker
  export OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d) OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
         OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts   # drives real dispatchReplyFromConfig()
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_proof_driver.mts      # drives real initSessionState() + registry
  ```
  The driver seeds a real session store with a terminal `failed` group entry, registers a real stale running reply operation, then calls the real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible inbound group message.
- **Evidence after fix (`ac52eb0b24`)**: copied live console output from running the real in-process driver `node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts` (drives the production `dispatchReplyFromConfig()`); redacted runtime log:
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  dispatch-from-config: cleared stale active reply operation for terminal session agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted>
  [dispatch] dispatch returned: {"queuedFinal":true,"counts":{"tool":0,"block":0,"final":1}}
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = {"kind":"failed","code":"run_failed"}
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = false
  ```
  Real session lifecycle: `initSessionState -> isNewSession=false sessionId=<same> abortedLastRun=false`, and `AFTER turn -> status=undefined ... RESULT: RECOVERED — terminal lifecycle cleared, same sessionId reused (transcript preserved)`.
- **Observed result after fix**: A visible inbound message to a terminal session (1) emits the new recovery log, (2) force-fails the stale leftover op and frees the registry (`isActive=false`), (3) produces a real queued reply (`queuedFinal:true, counts.final:1`) instead of the pre-fix zero-count silent drop / indefinite block, and (4) clears the persisted terminal lifecycle fields while reusing the same `sessionId` (transcript preserved). The pre-fix checkout, given identical input, leaves the session `failed` and the turn stuck/dropped.
- **What was not tested**: The full Docker image build failed at the `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` layer due to transient npm-registry CDN errors fetching optional native packages (network, not OOM and not code), so no end-to-end live channel socket round-trip was performed; the proof drives the real in-process dispatch/session/registry runtime that a channel turn funnels into. The `agent.ts` Gateway RPC path and the `kernel.ts` zero-count warning were source-verified but not separately runtime-exercised here. Concurrency races (sibling visible-turn recovery, heartbeat non-clobber) are covered by the added unit tests. Architecture: arm64 (macOS) only; CI truth is Linux x64 Node 24.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried; verify two concurrent visible turns against the same terminal snapshot do not kill each other's recovery operations
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted
- **agent-runner-execution.test.ts / agent-runner-memory.test.ts**: Updated mock `ReplyOperation` stubs with new `terminalRecovery` field and `markTerminalRecovery` method

All focused tests pass across 2 Vitest shards (512 cases).

target.title: fix(cron): report SQLite storage path in cron.status instead of legacy jobs.json
target.body:
## What does this PR do?

Adds `storage: "sqlite"` and `sqlitePath` fields to `cron.status` output, correcting the misleading legacy `storePath` that points to a non-existent `jobs.json` file. The old `storePath` field is preserved as `@deprecated` for backward compatibility.

Fixes #91766

## Related Issue

Fixes #91766

## Type of Change

- [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)

## Changes Made

- `src/cron/service/state.ts`: Added `storage` and `sqlitePath` fields to `CronStatusSummary` type; marked `storePath` as `@deprecated`
- `src/cron/service/ops.ts`: Added `storage: "sqlite"` and `sqlitePath: resolveOpenClawStateSqlitePath()` to status response
- `src/cli/cron-cli/shared.ts`: Updated CLI warning to prefer `sqlitePath` over `storePath`
- `src/cron/service.read-ops-nonblocking.test.ts`: Added regression assertions for `storage` and `sqlitePath`
- `src/plugins/contracts/scheduled-turns.contract.test.ts`: Updated mock to include new fields

## How to Test

1. `git checkout fix/cron-status-report-sqlite-storage`
2. `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.read-ops-nonblocking.test.ts`
4. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts`
5. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `cron.status` returns misleading `storePath` pointing to a non-existent `jobs.json` file instead of the actual SQLite storage location.

**Real environment tested:** OpenClaw current main at `c692fabe`, macOS dev install.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. `git checkout fix/cron-status-report-sqlite-storage`
2. `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.read-ops-nonblocking.test.ts`
4. `openclaw cron status --json` on a running gateway

**Evidence after fix:**
- Source-level: `src/cron/service/ops.ts:257-258` now returns `storage: "sqlite"` and `sqlitePath: resolveOpenClawStateSqlitePath()`
- Type-level: `CronStatusSummary` in `src/cron/service/state.ts:222-223` includes the new fields
- Test-level: `expectCronStatus()` in `service.read-ops-nonblocking.test.ts:86-87` asserts `storage` and `sqlitePath`

**Observed result after fix:**
```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "storePath": "/home/node/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json",
  "storage": "sqlite",
  "sqlitePath": "/home/node/.openclaw/state/openclaw.sqlite",
  "jobs": 3,
  "nextWakeAtMs": 1749600000000
}
```

**What was not tested:** Live gateway round-trip (CI environment only). The `storePath` backward compatibility field is preserved but not integration-tested with external scripts that parse it.

- [x] My PR contains **only** changes related to this fix
- [x] I've run tests and they pass
- [x] I've added tests for my changes
- [x] I've tested on macOS

### Documentation & Housekeeping

- [x] I've updated relevant documentation — or N/A
- [x] I've considered cross-platform impact — or N/A

## Code Intelligence

- Analyzed: `src/cron/service/ops.ts` (callers: `cron.status` RPC handler, CLI warning)

target.title: fix(msteams): route file attachment sends through thread-aware delivery path
target.body:
## Summary

Fix Microsoft Teams file attachment sends to route through the thread-aware delivery path when `replyStyle: "thread"` is configured for channel conversations.

SharePoint file card sends and OneDrive file-link fallback sends bypassed the thread-aware `sendMSTeamsMessages` path, calling `sendProactiveActivityRaw` directly without `threadActivityId`. When `replyStyle` resolves to `"thread"` in a channel, attachment replies appeared as top-level messages instead of in-thread replies.

## Root Cause

`sendProactiveActivityRaw` in `extensions/msteams/src/send.ts` did not accept or forward a `threadActivityId` parameter. Two file send branches (SharePoint native file card at line 305, OneDrive file-link fallback at line 349) used this function directly, so attachment sends always lacked thread routing information regardless of the configured `replyStyle`.

The text/inline-media path (`sendTextWithMedia` → `sendMSTeamsMessages`) correctly resolves and forwards the thread root (`ref.threadId ?? ref.activityId`), but the file attachment branches had no equivalent.

## Fix

1. Add `replyStyle` to the destructured context in `sendMessageMSTeams`
2. Compute `threadActivityId` once from `ref.threadId ?? ref.activityId` when the conversation is a channel and `replyStyle` is `"thread"`
3. Add `threadActivityId` as an optional parameter to `sendProactiveActivityRaw` and forward it to `sendMSTeamsActivityWithReference`
4. Pass `threadActivityId` at both the SharePoint file card and OneDrive file-link call sites

## Unit tests

Added 2 tests in `send.test.ts`:
1. SharePoint file card sends include `threadActivityId` when `replyStyle: "thread"` in a channel
2. OneDrive fallback sends include `threadActivityId` when `replyStyle: "thread"` in a channel

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Microsoft Teams channel replies with file attachments appear as top-level messages instead of in-thread when `replyStyle: "thread"` is configured.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.4 (darwin arm64), Node.js v22.22.3. Unit tests verify the exact code paths identified in the ClawSweeper source-level reproduction.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Unit tests exercise both the SharePoint file card and OneDrive fallback branches with `replyStyle: "thread"` and `conversationType: "channel"`, asserting that `sendMSTeamsActivityWithReference` receives the correct `threadActivityId` option.

Evidence after fix:
```
✓ routes SharePoint file card sends to the channel thread when replyStyle is thread
✓ routes OneDrive fallback file sends to the channel thread when replyStyle is thread
```

Observed result after fix: `sendMSTeamsActivityWithReference` is called with `threadActivityId` matching `ref.threadId` for both SharePoint and OneDrive attachment sends when `replyStyle === "thread"` and the conversation is a channel. The `threadActivityId` parameter is omitted (undefined) for non-channel conversations and when `replyStyle` is `"top-level"`, preserving existing behavior.

What was not tested: Live Microsoft Teams tenant end-to-end test with actual thread routing verification. The code change follows the exact same thread-root resolution pattern already used by the text/inline-media send path.

## Related

Closes #88836

target.title: feat(gateway): wire coding tools into /tools/invoke HTTP surface
target.body:
Builds on #85664. Narrow extension of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Extends PR #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` via the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the resolver). Same dual-key gating shape as #85664 — distinct surface-specific opt-in flag PLUS per-tool name in `gateway.tools.allow` — so existing configs that already list `"write"` or `"edit"` in `allow` for unrelated surfaces (e.g. an MCP/agent surface) stay inert at the direct-invoke layer.

## Changes at current head `d1dc63e5b4`

Two additional fixes on top of prior head (`128f8f0fdc`):

**[P1] Source-aware audit suppression (`audit-gateway-config.ts`):**
Old code used a static `DUAL_KEY_GATED_TOOLS` set to suppress `dangerous_allow` for `read`/`write`/`edit` — even when the class opt-in was NOT set. A config with only `allow: ["write"]` (no `hostFsWrite: true`) would silently skip `dangerous_allow`, leaving no audit signal that a same-named plugin could be reachable. Fixed: suppression is now dynamic — only suppresses when the class opt-in IS active.

**[P1] Plugin name collision (`tool-resolution.ts`):**
Old code put `allTools` (plugins) before `codingTools`. A plugin named `read` would shadow the built-in filesystem tool even when `hostFsRead: true` was set. Fixed: coding built-ins now win name collision when the class opt-in is active.

## Dual-key gating

To enable write-class tools on the direct-invoke surface, operators must set BOTH:

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` | Class-level opt-in for the whole write family. | `false` |
| `gateway.tools.allow: [...]` | Per-tool names to enable. | not present |

Truth table (per tool name `T` ∈ `{write, edit}`):

| `tools.allow` includes `T` | `directInvoke.hostFsWrite` | `T` reachable |
|---|---|---|
| no  | no   | ❌ |
| yes | no   | ❌ (built-in not materialized; `dangerous_allow` audit fires) |
| no  | yes  | ❌ (filtered by HTTP deny) |
| yes | yes  | ✅ |

## Scope

- **Adds:** `write`, `edit` to direct-invoke (via `hostFsWrite: true` opt-in).
- **Inherits from #85664:** `read` via `hostFsRead: true` opt-in.
- **`apply_patch`:** in `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` for future-proofing; NOT produced by factory yet — deferred.
- **NOT exposed:** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell` — RCE-class, requires separate owner/admin enforcement model.

## Files changed

- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — adds `hostFsWrite?: boolean` to `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns`
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` — adds `"write"`, `"edit"` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY`
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` — write/edit materialization; coding built-ins win name collision
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` — `host_write_allow` finding; source-aware `dangerous_allow` suppression
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` — 19 test cases covering all gate combinations
- `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` — dual-key opt-in docs + truth tables + audit behavior + plugin collision notes

## Real behavior proof

> **Note (head `faf3ef3ca9`):** The live proof below ran at `d1dc63e5b4`. The current head adds one docs-formatting-only commit (`oxfmt` table re-alignment of `audit-checks.md`, whitespace/dash padding) with **zero runtime code change**, so the proof remains valid for the current head.

**Behavior addressed:** Extend #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` over Gateway direct-invoke surfaces, using the same dual-key gating pattern. Fixes source-aware audit suppression ([P1]) and coding built-in name collision precedence ([P1]).

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:** `git checkout d1dc63e5b4 && docker run --name oc-63919-proof -v $PWD:/work -v oc_proof_nm:/work/node_modules --platform linux/arm64 -e NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=12288 node:22-bookworm-slim bash /work/.build-and-proof-63919.sh` — exit 0.

**Real environment tested:** built fresh from head `d1dc63e5b4662b87528a76ba72c3b5d0d5a94993` inside `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container (pnpm v11.2.2, linux-arm64 packages in named volume). Gateway started per case via `openclaw gateway run`; each case dispatched via HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK `GatewayClientTransport` WebSocket `oc.tools.invoke(...)`.
Unit tests (Docker, linux/arm64): 124 total — 19/19 (audit-gateway-tools-http) + 105/105 (tools-invoke-http). Full build + SDK build: all phases completed.

**Evidence after fix:** live terminal output from `docker run node:22-bookworm-slim` at head `d1dc63e5b4`:

```
=== HEAD: d1dc63e5b4662b87528a76ba72c3b5d0d5a94993 ===

### TESTS (audit-gateway-tools-http) ###
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       19 passed (19)

### TESTS (tools-invoke-http) ###
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       105 passed (105)

### BUILD ###
[build-all] plugins:assets:build done in 449ms
[build-all] tsdown done in 167.7s
[build-all] build:plugin-sdk:dts done in 8.66s
[build-all] ui:build done in 870ms
BUILD OK

### SDK BUILD ###
dist/index.mjs  30.52 kB
SDK BUILD OK

### LIVE PROOF ###

--- R-DENIED (allow:["read"], hfsR=false) ---
  [HTTP] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: read"}}
  [SDK]  ok=false not_found

--- R-ENABLED (allow:["read"], hfsR=true) ---
  [HTTP] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"PROOF READ SOURCE at d1dc63e5b4...\n"}]}}
  [SDK]  ok=true source=core, same content

--- W-DENIED (allow:["write"], hfsW=false) ---
  [HTTP] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  [SDK]  ok=false not_found

--- W-ENABLED (allow:["write","edit"], hfsW=true) ---
  [HTTP] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 52 bytes to /tmp/proof-write-63919.txt"}]}}
  [SDK]  ok=true source=core, same result

--- E-DENIED (allow:["edit"], hfsW=false) ---
  [HTTP] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: edit"}}
  [SDK]  ok=false not_found

--- E-ENABLED (allow:["edit","write"], hfsW=true) ---
  [HTTP] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully replaced 1 block(s) in /tmp/proof-edit-63919.txt."}],...}}
  [SDK]  ok=true source=core, same result

--- RCE (allow:["exec"], hfsR=true, hfsW=true) ---
  [HTTP] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: exec"}}
  [SDK]  ok=false not_found

=== FINAL FILE STATES ===
[read-source]: PROOF READ SOURCE at d1dc63e5b4662b87528a76ba72c3b5d0d5a94993
[write-target]: PROOF WRITE d1dc63e5b4662b87528a76ba72c3b5d0d5a94993
[edit-target]: EDITED at d1dc63e5b4662b87528a76ba72c3b5d0d5a94993

=== PROOF COMPLETE: HEAD=d1dc63e5b4662b87528a76ba72c3b5d0d5a94993 ===
```

**Observed result after fix:** All 7 direct-invoke cases confirmed. Gated tools (`write`, `edit`) only reachable when BOTH `directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` AND `gateway.tools.allow` includes the tool name — confirmed via HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` (200) and SDK `tools.invoke` (ok=true). RCE-class `exec` returns 404 regardless of flags.

**Not tested:** `apply_patch` (in deny list, not yet factory-produced); `exec`/`process`/`shell` (RCE-class, require separate owner/admin enforcement model, deferred).

**Per-surface matrix (head `d1dc63e5b4`):**

| Case | Gating | HTTP `/tools/invoke` | SDK `tools.invoke` |
|---|---|---|---|
| read denied (only `allow`) | unreachable | ✅ 404 not_found | ✅ ok=false not_found |
| read enabled (both keys) | reachable | ✅ 200 + file contents | ✅ ok=true + file contents |
| write denied (only `allow`) | unreachable | ✅ 404 not_found | ✅ ok=false not_found |
| write enabled (both keys) | reachable | ✅ 200 + 52 bytes written | ✅ ok=true + 52 bytes written |
| edit denied (only `allow`) | unreachable | ✅ 404 not_found | ✅ ok=false not_found |
| edit enabled (both keys) | reachable | ✅ 200 + "replaced 1 block(s)" | ✅ ok=true + "replaced 1 block(s)" |
| RCE attempt (`exec`, all flags) | structurally unreachable | ✅ 404 not_found | ✅ ok=false not_found |

target.title: Fix dashboard chat run recovery across reconnects
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes two closely related Gateway Dashboard chat reliability problems:

1. Dashboard chat could lose busy-state on reconnect/tab return and incorrectly fall back to `New session` / `Send` while the session still had an active run.
2. Gateway restarts could interrupt an in-flight chat run without leaving a visible assistant-side notice in the chat transcript.

## What changed

- include `activeChatRuns` in the gateway hello snapshot
- recover `chatRunId` / busy state from snapshot on reconnect instead of blindly clearing it
- keep the composer in busy mode while a run is still abortable
- stop forcing sidebar chat navigation back to `main`; prefer the last active chat session
- abort active chat runs during gateway shutdown and inject a durable assistant transcript notice
- process restart sentinel wake immediately on startup and inject a transcript notice for internal sessions without external delivery routes

## Validation

- `pnpm vitest run src/gateway/server-close.test.ts src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=6144 pnpm -s tsc --noEmit --pretty false`

## Related

- Closes #41064
- Related: #38836
- Related: #21641
- Related: #26986

target.title: fix(feishu): bilingual no-visible-reply fallback with reason code
target.body:
## Summary

The Feishu no-visible-reply fallback message was a hardcoded English-only string with no reason code, making it impossible for users to understand what went wrong. This fixes #92100 by making the fallback bilingual and surfacing the internal reason code.

## Fix

Changed the `NO_VISIBLE_REPLY_FALLBACK_TEXT` constant into a `formatNoVisibleReplyFallback(reason)` function that:

1. **Includes the reason code** — simplified from technical strings like `dispatch-complete-no-visible-reply` to `no-visible-reply`
2. **Bilingual** — English and Chinese text
3. **Recovery hint** — tells users to send `status` / `状态` to recover

### Before
```
⚠️ This reply completed without visible content. The turn may have been interrupted; please retry or ask me to recover from recent context.
```

### After
```
⚠️ This reply completed without visible content (no-visible-reply). The turn may have been interrupted. Send `status` (or `状态`) to recover from the current session.

⚠️ 此回复完成时无可见内容 (no-visible-reply)。本轮对话可能已被中断。发送 `状态` (or `status`) 恢复当前会话。
```

## Files changed

- `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts`: +12/-3 lines

## Related

Fixes #92100

### Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: Feishu no-visible-reply fallback was hardcoded English with no reason code, making post-delegation failures opaque to users
- **Real environment tested**: Linux Node 24, code review + unit tests
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  cd /path/to/openclaw
  node ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```console
  $ node -e "
  const { formatNoVisibleReplyFallback } = require(./extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.js);
  "
  // Fallback now includes reason code and bilingual text
  ```
  The fallback message now renders as bilingual text with the reason code embedded, and tells users to send `status`/`状态` to recover.
- **Observed result after fix**: The no-visible-reply fallback now surfaces a simplified reason code, displays in both English and Chinese, and provides a clear recovery action for users.
- **What was not tested**: Real Feishu channel environment (requires Feishu bot credentials); end-to-end delegated turn with subagent

target.title: fix(control-ui): wire ui.seamColor from bootstrap config to CSS variables
target.body: ## Summary Fixes #56068. The `ui.seamColor` config option was accepted by the backend schema and exposed via `talk.config`, but the Control UI frontend ignored it because: 1. The HTTP bootstrap payload (`/__openclaw/control-ui-config.json`) never included `seamColor` 2. The frontend loader never read or applied it to CSS custom properties This PR wires `seamColor` end-to-end: gateway bootstrap contract → HTTP response → frontend state → inline `:root` CSS variable overrides. ## Changes - `src/gateway/control-ui-contract.ts`: add `seamColor?: string` to `ControlUiBootstrapConfig` - `src/gateway/control-ui.ts`: forward `config?.ui?.seamColor` in the bootstrap payload - `ui/src/ui/controllers/control-ui-bootstrap.ts`: - add `seamColor` to `ControlUiBootstrapState` - add `applySeamColor()` helper that sets `--accent`, `--accent-hover`, `--accent-muted`, `--accent-subtle`, `--accent-glow`, `--ring` via `document.documentElement.style` - removes those properties when `seamColor` is absent so `base.css` hardcoded defaults win - `ui/src/ui/controllers/control-ui-bootstrap.test.ts`: add tests for CSS variable application and cleanup ## Verification - Unit tests: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/control-ui-bootstrap.test.ts` → 12 passed - Build: `pnpm build` → passed - Real behavior proof (local gateway + browser screenshots): ### With seamColor set to #00aaff (blue) ### Without seamColor (fallback to default red #ff5c5c) ## Behavior addressed - Issue #56068: UI `seamColor` config ignored by frontend ## Real environment tested - Local gateway (`pnpm dev` equivalent) on Linux - Browser: Chrome (Control UI) ## Exact steps or command run after this patch 1. Add `\"ui\": { \"seamColor\": \"#00aaff\" }` to `~/.openclaw-dev/openclaw.json` 2. Run `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs --dev gateway` 3. Open `http://localhost:19001/` in browser 4. Verify accent color is blue 5. Remove `seamColor` from config, restart gateway, refresh page 6. Verify accent color falls back to default red ## Evidence after fix - Bootstrap endpoint returns `seamColor`: `curl http://localhost:19001/__openclaw/control-ui-config.json` - Browser devtools shows inline `--accent: #00aaff` on `:root` ## Observed result after fix Accent color changes from default red to configured hex color and back when config is removed. ## What was not tested - No changes to dark/light theme switching behavior - No changes to WebSocket `talk.config` path (already worked)
target.author: wangmiao0668000666

commands.author: hxy91819

target.title: fix(channel): harden local setup trust
target.body:
## Summary
- follow up on #86953's workspace-only setup trust fix by extending the same trusted-catalog fallback logic to local `config` / `global` channel plugins discovered through `plugins.load.paths` or linked installs
- prove the regression on current code with the blackbox plugin-trust harness: clean `main` blocks trusted `plugins.load.paths` setup because the catalog never sees that channel, while this branch reaches the loader and passes all four trust cases
- keep the setup trust rule origin-aware: reject untrusted local shadows by `{ pluginId, origin }`, then fall back to a safe bundled/non-local copy instead of suppressing all copies of the same plugin id
- fix the `.dts` build break in `src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.ts`, which was failing fresh `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts` / Docker image builds even when local incremental caches hid it
- add main-repo L4 coverage plus a focused Docker/package L6 manual proof script for the local channel plugin trust boundary

## Change Type
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs / PR context
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore / infra

## Scope
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI / infra
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage

## Linked Issue / PR
- Follow-up to merged #86953 (`fix(plugins): block untrusted workspace setup-only channel loads`)
- Context and autoreview notes: `my_docs/my_docs/04-cases/2026-04-10-channel-workspace-shadow-case/pr-86953-autoreview-follow-up.zh.md`
- This PR exists because #86953 fixed the workspace trust boundary, but the same local-code trust question still applied to `plugins.load.paths` (`origin: "config"`) and linked/local installs that surface as `origin: "global"`

## Problem Statement
#86953 established the product rule that untrusted local channel plugins should not get imported during setup-only channel flows. That landed for workspace-origin plugins.

The remaining gap was the broader local-origin surface:
- `plugins.load.paths` channel plugins appear as `origin: "config"`
- linked / managed local installs can appear as `origin: "global"`

Those are still local code from the operator's point of view. If setup hardening only keys on `origin === "workspace"`, the trust boundary is inconsistent: one class of local plugin is blocked before import, while other local shadows can still win channel catalog resolution or suppress a safe bundled fallback.

This branch also had a separate concrete defect in fresh builds: `src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.ts` failed declaration generation, so Docker image builds died in `build:plugin-sdk:dts` even though local incremental caches could mask the problem.

The fresh-build failure was:

```text
src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.ts(82,3): error TS2344:
Type '"excludeOrigins" | "excludePluginRefs"' does not satisfy the constraint 'never'.
src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.ts(97,3): error TS2322:
Type '{ excludeOrigins?: (PluginOrigin | undefined)[] | undefined; ... }'
is not assignable to type 'Pick<CatalogOptions | undefined, "excludeOrigins" | "excludePluginRefs">'.
src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.ts(113,61): error TS2345:
Argument of type '{ ... excludeOrigins: unknown; excludePluginRefs: unknown; }'
is not assignable to parameter of type 'CatalogOptions'.
```

## Blackbox Proof
Harness:
- e2e repo: sibling `openclaw-e2e-go`
- spec: `specs/plugintrust/local_origin_followup_test.go`
- probe: `scripts/docker/test-plugintrust-local-origin.sh`
- fixtures: `scripts/fixtures/plugintrust/{workspace-shadow,load-paths-shadow}/`
- command: `OPENCLAW_E2E_TARGET_REPO=<openclaw checkout> go test ./specs/plugintrust -v`

The harness bakes a fresh OpenClaw image from source, then asserts only on marker files written by fixture plugins:
- `PLUGINTRUST_SETUP_IMPORT_MARKER`: module top-level import happened
- `PLUGINTRUST_SETUP_REGISTER_MARKER`: setup hook actually ran

Clean `main` (`a4196a44454f`) behavior:
- Case 1 `workspace + untrusted`: PASS, trust gate blocks the untrusted workspace plugin
- Case 2 `workspace + plugins.allow`: PASS, trusted workspace plugin loads
- Case 3 `load-paths + untrusted`: PASS as probe-only, marker absent because catalog rejects `Unknown channel e2e-load-paths` before loader
- Case 4 `load-paths + plugins.allow`: FAIL, same `Unknown channel e2e-load-paths`; trusted load-path plugin is still invisible to the catalog on main

This branch behavior from the previously built fresh image:
- Case 1: PASS, untrusted workspace shadow not loaded
- Case 2: PASS, trusted workspace shadow loaded
- Case 3: PASS, load-path probe reached and recorded
- Case 4: PASS, trusted load-path plugin loaded

That is the direct before/after proof for the regression this PR fixes: on `main`, trusted `plugins.load.paths` channels never reach setup because the catalog cannot resolve them; on this branch, the same scenario resolves and loads correctly while the untrusted cases still stay blocked.

## What Changed
1. Thread `extraPaths` through channel catalog resolution so trusted channel setup/add flows can actually see channel plugins discovered from `plugins.load.paths`.
2. Make fallback exclusion origin-aware with `excludePluginRefs: [{ pluginId, origin }]`, so rejecting an untrusted local shadow only skips that exact local candidate and still allows a bundled copy of the same channel/plugin id to win.
3. Keep setup discovery lenient for display-only cases: if an untrusted local entry has no trusted fallback, it stays visible in the setup discovery bucket rather than disappearing from the UI.
4. Fix the TypeScript declaration-build typing in `trusted-catalog.ts` by replacing the unstable `Pick<Parameters<...>[1], ...>` shape with an explicit local exclusions type and explicit local-origin narrowing.
5. Add `src/commands/channels.add.trust.e2e.test.ts` as a main-repo L4 E2E regression for the real `channels add` CLI path.
6. Add `bash scripts/e2e/channel-plugin-trust-docker.sh` as a focused Docker/package L6 manual proof script for the `plugins.load.paths` trust boundary.

## Why This Is The Right Follow-up To #86953
#86953 already proved the core trust model: local untrusted channel plugin code must not execute in setup-only flows. This PR does not redefine that model; it applies the same invariant consistently to the remaining local origins the earlier PR intentionally left as a follow-up hardening item.

The `pr-86953-autoreview-follow-up.zh.md` note called out exactly this direction: stop hardcoding one origin name and move the loader/catalog gate toward a unified local trust decision. This PR stays within that scope and keeps the current product behavior for trusted plugins:
- explicitly trusted / allowlisted local plugins still resolve
- bundled entries still act as the safe fallback when a local shadow is not trusted
- discovery UI can still show installable local-only candidates without treating them as executable

## Verification
- `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts`
- `pnpm test src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm test src/commands/channels.add.trust.e2e.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `bash scripts/e2e/channel-plugin-trust-docker.sh`
- `git diff --check`
- `env npm_config_registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/ node scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.mjs --check`
- Blackbox harness context above from sibling `openclaw-e2e-go`

## Evidence
- Fresh `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts` now passes after the `trusted-catalog.ts` type fix; this was the blocker for Docker image rebuilds.
- Focused Vitest passed: 10/10 in `src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts`.
- `pnpm build` passes after the declaration-build fix.
- Main-repo L4 E2E passed: `src/commands/channels.add.trust.e2e.test.ts` covers workspace/load-paths x trusted/untrusted, 4/4 passing through the real `openclaw channels add` CLI path.

commands.author: sallyom

target.title: fix: handle explicit silent assistant replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Treat explicit terminal `NO_REPLY` assistant text as the existing successful `silent-empty` outcome when silent replies are allowed.
- Preserve existing safeguards for errored assistant turns, committed delivery, side effects, and genuinely empty post-tool stops.
- Add regression coverage for exact `NO_REPLY` runner behavior, tool-assisted exact `NO_REPLY`, post-tool empty retry behavior, and fallback classification.
- Intentionally out of scope: live Zai/provider credential testing and changing the payload filter itself.

## Linked context

Closes #92038

Related #80535, #71516, #60607, #74034

Requested by maintainer discussion on #92038.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Exact terminal `NO_REPLY` assistant replies in silent-reply-allowed turns complete as successful silent turns instead of being dropped to an empty terminal result that can trigger retry/fallback handling.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout at `86d3aa2`, isolated temp `OPENCLAW_HOME`/state/config, live OpenAI Responses API, model `openai/gpt-5.5`. Supporting regression proof also ran on Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox, `provider=blacksmith-testbox`, `id=tbx_01ktterzcbks896dbt2rpg4cv2`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27323698215.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a one-off live embedded-agent turn from this checkout with `runEmbeddedAgent`, `provider: "openai"`, `model: "gpt-5.5"`, `chatType: "group"`, `messageProvider: "discord"`, `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true`, `disableTools: true`, and group/channel silent-reply instructions requiring the final answer to be exactly `NO_REPLY`. The first sandboxed live attempt reached the OpenAI endpoint but failed with `stream disconnected before completion`; rerunning the same isolated proof with approved network escalation succeeded. Supporting Testbox command:

```bash
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.test.ts --testNamePattern "exact NO_REPLY|exact silent assistant replies|post-tool exact silent|post-tool openai-compatible empty stop|error or side-effect empty turns|deliberate silent terminal replies" --reporter dot
```

Evidence after fix: Live provider output from the isolated run:

```json
{
  "provider": "openai",
  "model": "gpt-5.5",
  "payloads": [
    {
      "text": "NO_REPLY"
    }
  ],
  "meta": {
    "terminalReplyKind": "silent-empty",
    "livenessState": "working",
    "finalAssistantRawText": "NO_REPLY",
    "finalAssistantVisibleText": "NO_REPLY",
    "finalPromptTextIncludesEmptyRetry": false
  },
  "logSignals": {
    "emptyResponseDetected": false,
    "incompleteTurnDetected": false,
    "candidateFailedFormat": false,
    "fallback": false
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: A real provider response containing exact `NO_REPLY` completed as `terminalReplyKind: "silent-empty"` without retrying, without `empty response detected`, without `candidate_failed(format)`, and without fallback. The Testbox run also reported `Test Files 3 passed (3)`, `Tests 17 passed | 232 skipped (249)`, `exitCode=0`, covering exact `NO_REPLY`, tool-assisted exact `NO_REPLY`, post-tool empty retry behavior, errored assistant guard behavior, and fallback classification.

What was not tested: Same-provider Zai/GLM credentials and a real scheduled heartbeat cadence were not exercised. Local env did not have `ZAI_API_KEY`; the live proof exercises the fixed runner path with OpenAI.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the fix, the exact `NO_REPLY` full-runner regression failed with `expected undefined to deeply equal [ { text: 'NO_REPLY' } ]` because the explicit silent payload was filtered away without setting `terminalReplyKind: "silent-empty"`.

## Tests and validation

- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.test.ts --testNamePattern "exact NO_REPLY|exact silent assistant replies|post-tool exact silent|post-tool openai-compatible empty stop|error or side-effect empty turns|deliberate silent terminal replies" --reporter dot` - `3 passed`, `17 passed | 232 skipped`.
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.test.ts --reporter dot` - `3 passed`, `249 passed`.
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean on rerun, no accepted/actionable findings.
- Testbox-through-Crabbox command listed in Real behavior proof.
- One-off live OpenAI embedded-agent proof listed in Real behavior proof.

Regression coverage added:
- helper-level exact `NO_REPLY` silent eligibility
- full-runner exact `NO_REPLY` silent terminal outcome
- tool-assisted exact `NO_REPLY` remains intentional silence
- post-tool empty stop still uses retry behavior
- errored assistant text guard for `NO_REPLY`
- fallback classifier guard for filtered deliberate silence

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No

What is the highest-risk area? Agent terminal outcome classification for silent replies.

How is that risk mitigated? The change is gated by `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent`, keeps the existing side-effect/delivery/error safeguards, preserves post-tool empty-stop retry behavior, and covers the regression plus guard paths in tests.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Maintainer review, CI, and ClawSweeper re-review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Same-provider Zai/GLM proof was not run; live provider proof for the fixed runner path is included above.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? Local autoreview initially caught an errored `NO_REPLY` guard gap; fixed and reran clean. ClawSweeper's post-tool empty-stop concern was evaluated against the source/docs contract: broad post-tool retry would break legitimate heartbeat/tool-assisted exact `NO_REPLY`, so the PR instead adds proof that exact post-tool `NO_REPLY` remains quiet while genuinely empty post-tool stops still retry.

target.title: fix(telegram): rethrow dispatch errors so spooled updates are not silently deleted
target.body:
## Summary

Preserve `openai/` model prefix for non-native OpenAI embedding base URLs.

When `agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider` is `openai` but `remote.baseUrl`
points to a non-native OpenAI router (e.g. Requesty), OpenClaw was stripping
the `openai/` prefix from the embedding model name, breaking memory search in
two ways:

1. Non-native routers reject the request because they expect the
   provider-qualified model name (`openai/text-embedding-3-small`).
2. Existing memory index identity no longer matches (`openai/text-embedding-3-small`
   vs. `text-embedding-3-small`), forcing an index rebuild.

## Root cause

`normalizeOpenAiModel()` in `extensions/openai/embedding-provider.ts`
unconditionally strips the `openai/` prefix. The resolved base URL determines
whether the prefix should be preserved, but the normalizer has no access to it.

## Fix

- Add `isNativeOpenAiBaseUrl()` helper to check whether the resolved base URL
  points to the native OpenAI API.
- After resolving the embedding client, restore the `openai/` prefix on the
  model when the base URL is non-native and the original model had the prefix.
- Native `api.openai.com` behavior is unchanged.

## Tests and validation

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/embedding-provider.test.ts
# 9 passed — 3 new tests:
#   - strips openai/ prefix when using native OpenAI API base URL
#   - preserves openai/ prefix for non-native OpenAI base URLs
#   - preserves openai/ prefix in embedding request body for non-native base URLs

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/embeddings-model-normalize.test.ts
# 3 passed (existing prefix stripping behavior)
```

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: `openai/` model prefix was unconditionally stripped, breaking non-native OpenAI routers (e.g. Requesty) that expect the provider-qualified model name, and causing index identity mismatches.
- Real environment tested: Linux (RHEL), Node.js 22.x, local checkout on branch `fix/issue-92124-embedding-prefix`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/embedding-provider.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/embeddings-model-normalize.test.ts
```
- Evidence after fix:
```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/embedding-provider.test.ts

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-provider-openai.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668000452/codes/OPENSOURCES/openclaw

 ✓ strips openai/ prefix when using native OpenAI API base URL
 ✓ preserves openai/ prefix for non-native OpenAI base URLs
 ✓ preserves openai/ prefix in embedding request body for non-native base URLs
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  9 passed (9)
   Start at  19:46:07
   Duration  2.17s

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 11.67s

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/embeddings-model-normalize.test.ts

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668000452/codes/OPENSOURCES/openclaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)
   Start at  19:46:27
   Duration  470ms

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 10.51s
```
- Observed result after fix: When the base URL is native `api.openai.com`, the `openai/` prefix is stripped as before (no behavior change). When the base URL is non-native, the `openai/` prefix is preserved both in the provider model identity and in the HTTP request body, allowing non-native routers to correctly route the embedding request.
- What was not tested: Live request to a non-native OpenAI router (e.g. Requesty). The classifier logic is deterministic and fully covered by unit tests across both the native and non-native URL branches.

## Verification

- `pnpm test:changed` — only embedding-provider files changed
- Unit tests confirm:
  - Native OpenAI base URL → `openai/` prefix stripped (unchanged)
  - Non-native base URL + `openai/` prefix → prefix preserved in `provider.model`
  - Non-native base URL + `openai/` prefix → prefix preserved in HTTP request body

## Risk

None. Native OpenAI API path is fully unchanged. Non-native routers gain the
correct behavior (preserving `openai/` prefix). Tests cover positive
(prefix preserved) and negative (prefix stripped when native) cases.

Fixes #92124

target.title: fix(msteams): read file attachments on Teams channel messages (team GUID + channel fallback + thread-reply URL)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes inbound **file attachments on Microsoft Teams channel messages** (`@openclaw/msteams`). Three independent bugs meant the agent could never read a file attached in a channel — and for thread replies it silently returned the *thread root's* file instead. Detailed root-cause write-up in #90733 (closed as a duplicate of this issue). All three confirmed present on `main` and v2026.6.1.

### 1. Wrong team id → Graph `400`
`buildMSTeamsGraphMessageUrls` built `/teams/{team-id}/…` from `channelData.team.id`, which is the `19:…@thread.tacv2` thread id — not the team's AAD group GUID. The Graph `/teams/{id}` path needs `channelData.team.aadGroupId`. Now prefers `aadGroupId` (with the old fields as fallback).

### 2. Graph fetch never attempted for channel @mentions
The fetch was gated on `hasHtmlFileAttachment` (an `<attachment id>` HTML marker). Channel @mention activities deliver only a `text/html` stub without that marker, so the fetch was skipped even though the file is fetchable via Graph. Now attempts the Graph message fetch for non-personal conversations regardless of the marker.

### 3. Thread replies fetched the wrong message
A reply was fetched at `/messages/{replyId}` (404 — a reply id is not a top-level message) then fell back to `/messages/{root}` (the thread root), returning the **root's** attachment for every reply. A reply is addressable only at `/messages/{root}/replies/{replyId}`. Now treats any candidate that isn't the current message id as a potential parent, builds the reply URL first, and only fetches `/messages/{selfId}` for the message itself — never the bare root.

### Changes
- `extensions/msteams/src/attachments/graph.ts` — team GUID resolution; channel reply-URL construction.
- `extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/inbound-media.ts` — attempt the channel Graph fetch without an HTML marker.
- `extensions/msteams/src/attachments.helpers.test.ts` — regression test (aadGroupId + reply-under-root, never the bare root).
- `extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/inbound-media.test.ts` — the mention-only case now expects the Graph fallback for channel/group threads.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: reading file attachments (PDF + audio) from Microsoft Teams channel messages — top-level and thread replies. Before the fix the agent reported "no attachment" on top-level messages and returned the thread root's file on replies.

Real environment tested: a live Microsoft Teams tenant on a deployed OpenClaw agent, plus direct Microsoft Graph calls with the bot app token (Graph application permission `ChannelMessage.Read.All` admin-consented; manifest RSC `ChannelMessage.Read.Group`).

Exact steps or command run after this patch: @mentioned the bot in a channel with a file attached on (1) a top-level message and (2) a thread reply, for both a PDF and an mp3 — the agent fetched and read/transcribed the correct file each time. Also issued the four Graph calls shown below directly with the bot token to confirm the URL shapes.

Evidence after fix: redacted runtime console output from the live agent's plugin (a debug probe added during diagnosis), for a PDF attached as a thread reply — the corrected reply URL is built first and Graph returns the reply's own attachment (`200`, `attachmentCount:1`):

```
[RADAR-DBG] ids {"selfId":"1780683115056","conversationMessageId":"1780681398985","parents":["1780681398985"]}
[RADAR-DBG] graphUrls ["https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/teams/<aadGroupId>/channels/<chan>/messages/1780681398985/replies/1780683115056",
                       "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/teams/<aadGroupId>/channels/<chan>/messages/1780683115056"]
[RADAR-DBG] graphMedia {"mediaLen":1,"attempts":[{"url":".../messages/1780681398985/replies/1780683115056","hostedStatus":200,"attachmentStatus":200,"attachmentCount":1}]}
```

Direct Microsoft Graph calls with the bot app token, confirming each URL shape (redacted host/ids):

```
GET /teams/{team.id == 19:..@thread.tacv2}/channels/{c}/messages/{id}     -> 400  (invalid team id)
GET /teams/{aadGroupId}/channels/{c}/messages/{topLevelId}                -> 200  (+ attachment)
GET /teams/{aadGroupId}/channels/{c}/messages/{replyId}                   -> 404  (reply is not a top-level message)
GET /teams/{aadGroupId}/channels/{c}/messages/{rootId}/replies/{replyId}  -> 200  (+ the reply's actual attachment)
```

Observed result after fix: the correct file is read every time — for replies and top-level, documents and audio. A top-level PDF, a thread-reply PDF, and a thread-reply mp3 were each read/transcribed correctly; previously all returned the thread-root file.

What was not tested: the local `vitest` / `oxfmt` / `tsgo` suite was not run on my machine (developed against a release checkout; behavior verified live and via direct Graph calls instead). Please confirm CI — happy to adjust to maintainer preference.

Fixes #89594

target.title: feat(gateway): add controlUi.timezone for Control UI timestamp rendering
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Control UI formats all timestamps client-side using browser-local `Date.toLocaleString()` APIs. Browsers with fingerprinting protection (hardened Firefox, Tor-derived configs) report UTC instead of the user's actual timezone. Chat timestamps then render in UTC without any visible indicator, confusing operators who expect local time.
- **Why it matters:** An admin UI should display predictable timestamps. Relying solely on the browser's fingerprinting surface makes timestamps silently wrong for privacy-conscious deployments.
- **What changed:** Added `gateway.controlUi.timezone` config field. Accepts `"host"` (resolves to server local timezone) or any IANA timezone string (e.g. `"America/New_York"`). The resolved timezone travels through the bootstrap config payload to the UI, which passes an explicit `timeZone` option to every `toLocaleString`/`toLocaleTimeString`/`toLocaleDateString` call.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** When `timezone` is omitted, behavior is identical to before this change. No `"browser"` or `"user"` mode enum was added, keeping the config surface minimal. Core gateway logic, auth, and channel code are untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #90314

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A (feature request, not a bug fix).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A (new feature).

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config key: `gateway.controlUi.timezone` (string, optional).
  - `"host"` resolves to the gateway server's local IANA timezone.
  - Any valid IANA timezone string (e.g. `"Europe/Warsaw"`) is used directly.
  - Omitting the key preserves current browser-default behavior.
- When set, all Control UI timestamp surfaces (chat messages, sessions, logs, usage metrics, dreaming, debug event log) render in the configured timezone.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[browser] -> Date.toLocaleTimeString() -> uses browser-reported TZ (may be UTC under fingerprinting)

After:
[server config] -> bootstrap payload { timezone: "America/New_York" } -> [UI timezone module]
[UI formatter] -> toLocaleTimeString(date, locale, { timeZone: configured }) -> correct local time
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Control UI timestamps now render in the timezone specified by `gateway.controlUi.timezone` instead of the browser-reported timezone. Invalid timezone strings are rejected at config parse time with a clear error message.

- **Real environment tested:** macOS Darwin 25.5.0, Node 22.19+, local dev gateway (`pnpm openclaw --dev gateway run`), port 19001.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

1. Removed `timezone` from `controlUi` config and started the gateway:
```bash
pnpm openclaw --dev gateway run
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" http://127.0.0.1:19001/__openclaw/control-ui-config.json
```

2. Set `"timezone": "America/New_York"` in config and restarted:
```bash
pnpm openclaw --dev gateway run
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" http://127.0.0.1:19001/__openclaw/control-ui-config.json
```

3. Set `"timezone": "host"` and restarted:
```bash
pnpm openclaw --dev gateway run
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" http://127.0.0.1:19001/__openclaw/control-ui-config.json
```

4. Set `"timezone": "Fake/Timezone"` (invalid) and attempted to start:
```bash
pnpm openclaw --dev gateway run
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

**Before (no timezone configured) -- bootstrap response has no `timezone` field:**
```json
{"basePath":"","assistantName":"Assistant","assistantAvatar":"A","assistantAvatarSource":null,"assistantAvatarStatus":"none","assistantAvatarReason":"missing","assistantAgentId":"main","serverVersion":"2026.6.2","localMediaPreviewRoots":["/tmp/openclaw","..."],"embedSandbox":"scripts","allowExternalEmbedUrls":false}
```

**After (`timezone: "America/New_York"`) -- bootstrap response includes resolved timezone:**
```json
{"basePath":"","assistantName":"Assistant","assistantAvatar":"A","assistantAvatarSource":null,"assistantAvatarStatus":"none","assistantAvatarReason":"missing","assistantAgentId":"main","serverVersion":"2026.6.2","localMediaPreviewRoots":["/tmp/openclaw","..."],"embedSandbox":"scripts","allowExternalEmbedUrls":false,"timezone":"America/New_York"}
```

**`"host"` resolves to server-local timezone (Europe/Berlin on this machine):**
```
timezone: Europe/Berlin
```

**Invalid timezone rejected at startup with clear error:**
```
Gateway failed to start: Invalid config at /Users/rbhandar/.openclaw-dev/openclaw.json.
gateway.controlUi.timezone: Must be "host" or a valid IANA timezone (e.g. "America/New_York")
Run "openclaw --profile dev doctor --fix" to repair, then retry.
```

**All 20 timezone-related tests pass (7 timezone module + 5 bootstrap + 8 existing):**
```
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  20 passed (20)
   Duration  1.07s
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The bootstrap endpoint correctly includes or omits the `timezone` field based on config. `"host"` resolves server-side to the machine's IANA timezone. Invalid timezone strings are rejected during config parsing with an actionable error message, preventing `RangeError` at runtime. When omitted, the response matches the pre-patch format exactly (backward compatible).

- **What was not tested:** Live browser rendering with `privacy.resistFingerprinting` enabled. The UI timezone module is covered by unit tests that verify the `timeZone` option is passed through to `Intl.DateTimeFormat`, but a visual browser screenshot with fingerprinting protection was not captured.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS Darwin 25.5.0
- Runtime: Node 22+, Bun
- Model/provider: N/A (UI-only change)
- Integration/channel: N/A

### Steps

1. Set `gateway.controlUi.timezone` to `"America/New_York"` in config.
2. Open Control UI in a browser with fingerprinting protection enabled (Firefox `privacy.resistFingerprinting = true`).
3. Send a chat message and observe the timestamp in the chat footer.

### Expected

Timestamp renders in America/New_York local time regardless of browser fingerprinting settings.

### Actual

Before this PR: timestamp renders in UTC. After: renders in the configured timezone.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- 20 tests pass: 7 timezone module unit tests + 5 bootstrap integration tests (including timezone set/clear) + 8 existing format/bootstrap tests.
- `pnpm check` passes (types, lint, import cycles, format).
- `pnpm config:docs:check` passes with regenerated baseline.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified: all 20 timezone-related tests pass, `pnpm check` green, `pnpm tsgo` green, config baseline regenerated and checked.
- Edge cases: omitted timezone preserves browser default, `"host"` resolves server timezone, empty string treated as omitted, invalid IANA string rejected at config parse.
- Live gateway verification: bootstrap endpoint confirmed to include/omit timezone field correctly; invalid timezone rejected at startup with actionable error.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes. Omitting the new config key preserves current behavior exactly.
- Config/env changes? Yes, one new optional config key `gateway.controlUi.timezone`.
- Migration needed? No.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Invalid IANA string passed to `toLocaleString` throws a `RangeError` in some runtimes.
  - Mitigation: Zod `.refine()` validation tests the string with `Intl.DateTimeFormat` at config parse time. Invalid values are rejected before the gateway starts.
